and"}}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":", Community Magistrate,","#text":"it is proved that the person passing the sentence or issuing the process acted as such a court under colour of having some appointment or commission lawfully authorising him or her to act as such a court, or, as the case may require, that the person issuing the warrant acted as a Justiceor other person having authority to do so."}}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"This section shall apply notwithstanding that, in fact,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any such appointment or commission as aforesaid did not exist or had expired; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the court or the person passing the sentence or issuing the process was not the court or the person authorised by the appointment or commission to act; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the person issuing the warrant was not duly authorised to issue it."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 48","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 28(1)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 June 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 206","@_href":"DLM297136","@_id":"DLM822196"},"amending-leg":"Corrections Act 2004","#text":":, on, byof the(2004 No 50).","@_id":"DLM328239"},{"amended-provision":"Section 28(1)﻿(a)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"30 June 1998","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 3(a)","@_href":"DLM428121","@_id":"DLM2073261"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 2) 1998","#text":":, on, byof the(1998 No 79).","@_id":"DLM328240"},{"amended-provision":"Section 28(1)﻿(b)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"30 June 1998","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 3(b)","@_href":"DLM428121","@_id":"DLM2073262"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 2) 1998","#text":":, on, byof the(1998 No 79).","@_id":"DLM328241"}]}},"@_id":"DLM328238","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Irregular warrant or process
sections.fullText: 29 Irregular warrant or process 1 Every one acting under a warrant or process that is bad in law on account of some defect in substance or in form, apparent on the face of it, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the warrant or process were good in law if in good faith and without culpable ignorance or negligence he or she believed that the warrant or process was good in law; and ignorance of the law shall in this case be an excuse. 2 It is a question of law whether the facts of which there is evidence do or do not constitute culpable ignorance or negligence in his or her so believing the warrant or process to be good in law. 1908 No 32 s 50
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"29","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Irregular warrant or process","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one acting under a warrant or process that is bad in law on account of some defect in substance or in form, apparent on the face of it, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the warrant or process were good in law if in good faith and without culpable ignorance or negligence he or she believed that the warrant or process was good in law; and ignorance of the law shall in this case be an excuse."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"It is a question of law whether the facts of which there is evidence do or do not constitute culpable ignorance or negligence in his or her so believing the warrant or process to be good in law."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 50","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328242","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arresting the wrong person
sections.fullText: 30 Arresting the wrong person 1 Every one duly authorised to execute a warrant to arrest who thereupon arrests a person, believing in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds that he or she is the person named in the warrant, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the person arrested had been the person named in the warrant. 2 Every one called on to assist the person making such arrest, and believing that the person in whose arrest he or she is called on to assist is the person for whose arrest the warrant is issued, and everywho is required to receive and detain the person arrested, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the person arrested had been the person named in the warrant. 1908 No 32 s 49 :, on, byof the(2004 No 50).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"30","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arresting the wrong person","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one duly authorised to execute a warrant to arrest who thereupon arrests a person, believing in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds that he or she is the person named in the warrant, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the person arrested had been the person named in the warrant."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":"prison manager","#text":"Every one called on to assist the person making such arrest, and believing that the person in whose arrest he or she is called on to assist is the person for whose arrest the warrant is issued, and everywho is required to receive and detain the person arrested, shall be protected from criminal responsibility to the same extent and subject to the same provisions as if the person arrested had been the person named in the warrant."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 49","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 30(2)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 June 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 206","@_href":"DLM297136","@_id":"DLM822401"},"amending-leg":"Corrections Act 2004","#text":":, on, byof the(2004 No 50).","@_id":"DLM328245"}}},"@_id":"DLM328244","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest by constable pursuant to statutory powers
sections.fullText: 31 Arrest by constable pursuant to statutory powers Every constable is justified in arresting any person without warrant in accordance with the provisions ofor in accordance with any other enactment conferring on him or her a power so to arrest. 1908 No 32 ss 56, 57(2)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"31","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest by constable pursuant to statutory powers","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 315","@_href":"DLM331230","@_id":"DLM822404"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"Every constable is justified in arresting any person without warrant in accordance with the provisions ofor in accordance with any other enactment conferring on him or her a power so to arrest."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 ss 56, 57(2)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328246","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest by constable of person believed to have committed offence
sections.fullText: 32 Arrest by constable of person believed to have committed offence Where under any enactment any constable has power to arrest without warrant any person who has committed an offence, the constable is justified in arresting without warrant any person whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have committed that offence, whether or not the offence has in fact been committed, and whether or not the arrested person committed it. 1908 No 32 s 51
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"32","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest by constable of person believed to have committed offence","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Where under any enactment any constable has power to arrest without warrant any person who has committed an offence, the constable is justified in arresting without warrant any person whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have committed that offence, whether or not the offence has in fact been committed, and whether or not the arrested person committed it."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 51","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328247","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest by other officers or persons pursuant to statutory powers
sections.fullText: 33 Arrest by other officers or persons pursuant to statutory powers Every officer or other person, not being a constable, who is authorised by any enactment to arrest any person without warrant is justified in so arresting any person in accordance with the provisions of that enactment.
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"33","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest by other officers or persons pursuant to statutory powers","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every officer or other person, not being a constable, who is authorised by any enactment to arrest any person without warrant is justified in so arresting any person in accordance with the provisions of that enactment."}]}]},"@_id":"DLM328248","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Persons assisting constable or officer in arrest
sections.fullText: 34 Persons assisting constable or officer in arrest 1 Every one called upon by a constable to assist him or her in the arrest of any person believed or suspected to have committed any offence is justified in assisting unless he or she knows that there is no reasonable ground for the belief or suspicion. 2 Where by any enactment it is provided that any officer or person, not being a constable, may call upon any other person to assist him or her in arresting without warrant any one who has committed or is found committing any offence, every person so called upon is justified in assisting unless he or she knows that there is no reasonable ground for believing that the person to be arrested has committed the offence. 1908 No 32 s 52
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"34","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Persons assisting constable or officer in arrest","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one called upon by a constable to assist him or her in the arrest of any person believed or suspected to have committed any offence is justified in assisting unless he or she knows that there is no reasonable ground for the belief or suspicion."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Where by any enactment it is provided that any officer or person, not being a constable, may call upon any other person to assist him or her in arresting without warrant any one who has committed or is found committing any offence, every person so called upon is justified in assisting unless he or she knows that there is no reasonable ground for believing that the person to be arrested has committed the offence."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 52","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328249","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest of persons found committing certain crimes
sections.fullText: 35 Arrest of persons found committing certain crimes Every one is justified in arresting without warrant— a any person whom he or she finds committing any offence against this Actfor which the maximum punishment is not less than 3 years' imprisonment: b any person whom he or she finds by night committing any offence against this Act. 1908 No 32 ss 53, 57(1) :, on, byof the(2008 No 8).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"35","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest of persons found committing certain crimes","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is justified in arresting without warrant—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"struckoutwords":"","#text":"any person whom he or she finds committing any offence against this Actfor which the maximum punishment is not less than 3 years' imprisonment:"}}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any person whom he or she finds by night committing any offence against this Act."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 ss 53, 57(1)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 35(a)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"27 March 2008","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 4","@_href":"DLM1209107","@_id":"DLM1238427"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2008","#text":":, on, byof the(2008 No 8).","@_id":"DLM1238424"}}},"@_id":"DLM328250","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest of person believed to be committing crime by night
sections.fullText: 36 Arrest of person believed to be committing crime by night Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting without warrant any person whom he or she finds by night in circumstances affording reasonable and probable grounds for believing that that person is committing an offence against this Act. 1908 No 32 s 55
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"36","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest of person believed to be committing crime by night","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting without warrant any person whom he or she finds by night in circumstances affording reasonable and probable grounds for believing that that person is committing an offence against this Act."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 55","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328251","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest after commission of certain crimes
sections.fullText: 37 Arrest after commission of certain crimes Where any offence against this Act has been committed, every one who believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, that any person has committed that offence is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting that person without warrant, whether or not that person committed the offence. 1908 No 32 s 54
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"37","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest after commission of certain crimes","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Where any offence against this Act has been committed, every one who believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, that any person has committed that offence is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting that person without warrant, whether or not that person committed the offence."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 54","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328252","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Arrest during flight
sections.fullText: 38 Arrest during flight 1 Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting without warrant any person whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have committed an offence against this Act, and to be escaping from and to be freshly pursued by any one whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have lawful authority to arrest that person for the offence. 2 This section shall apply whether or not the offence has in fact been committed, and whether or not the arrested person committed it. 1908 No 32 s 58
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"38","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Arrest during flight","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for arresting without warrant any person whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have committed an offence against this Act, and to be escaping from and to be freshly pursued by any one whom he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to have lawful authority to arrest that person for the offence."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"This section shall apply whether or not the offence has in fact been committed, and whether or not the arrested person committed it."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 58","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328253","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Force used in executing process or in arrest
sections.fullText: 39 Force used in executing process or in arrest Where any person is justified, or protected from criminal responsibility, in executing or assisting to execute any sentence, warrant, or process, or in making or assisting to make any arrest, that justification or protection shall extend and apply to the use by him or her of such force as may be necessary to overcome any force used in resisting such execution or arrest, unless the sentence, warrant, or process can be executed or the arrest made by reasonable means in a less violent manner: provided that, except in the case of a constable or a person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, this section shall not apply where the force used is intended or likely to cause death or grievous bodily harm. 1908 No 32 s 60
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"39","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Force used in executing process or in arrest","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Where any person is justified, or protected from criminal responsibility, in executing or assisting to execute any sentence, warrant, or process, or in making or assisting to make any arrest, that justification or protection shall extend and apply to the use by him or her of such force as may be necessary to overcome any force used in resisting such execution or arrest, unless the sentence, warrant, or process can be executed or the arrest made by reasonable means in a less violent manner:"},{"text":"provided that, except in the case of a constable or a person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, this section shall not apply where the force used is intended or likely to cause death or grievous bodily harm."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 60","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328255","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Preventing escape or rescue
sections.fullText: 40 Preventing escape or rescue 1 Where any person is lawfully authorised to arrest or to assist in arresting any other person, or is justified in or protected from criminal responsibility for arresting or assisting to arrest any other person, that authority, justification, or protection, as the case may be, shall extend and apply to the use of such force as may be necessary— unless in any such case the escape or rescue can be prevented by reasonable means in a less violent manner: a to prevent the escape of that other person if he or she takes to flight in order to avoid arrest; or b to prevent the escape or rescue of that other person after his or her arrest— provided that, except in the case of a constable or a person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, this subsection shall not apply where the force used is intended or likely to cause death or grievous bodily harm. 2 Where anyof ais attempting to escape from lawful custody, or is fleeing after having escaped therefrom, every constable, and every person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, is justified in using such force as may be necessary to prevent the escape of or to recapture the, unless in any such case the escape can be prevented or the recapture effected by reasonable means in a less violent manner. 1908 No 32 ss 62, 63, 64, 65 :, on, byof the(2004 No 50).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"40","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Preventing escape or rescue","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":["Where any person is lawfully authorised to arrest or to assist in arresting any other person, or is justified in or protected from criminal responsibility for arresting or assisting to arrest any other person, that authority, justification, or protection, as the case may be, shall extend and apply to the use of such force as may be necessary—","unless in any such case the escape or rescue can be prevented by reasonable means in a less violent manner:"],"label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to prevent the escape of that other person if he or she takes to flight in order to avoid arrest; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to prevent the escape or rescue of that other person after his or her arrest—"}]}]},{"text":"provided that, except in the case of a constable or a person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, this subsection shall not apply where the force used is intended or likely to cause death or grievous bodily harm."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":["prisoner","prison","prisoner"],"#text":"Where anyof ais attempting to escape from lawful custody, or is fleeing after having escaped therefrom, every constable, and every person called upon by a constable to assist him or her, is justified in using such force as may be necessary to prevent the escape of or to recapture the, unless in any such case the escape can be prevented or the recapture effected by reasonable means in a less violent manner."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 ss 62, 63, 64, 65","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 40(2)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 June 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 206","@_href":"DLM297136","@_id":"DLM822405"},"amending-leg":"Corrections Act 2004","#text":":, on, byof the(2004 No 50).","@_id":"DLM328257"}}},"@_id":"DLM328256","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Prevention of suicide or certain offences
sections.fullText: 41 Prevention of suicide or certain offences 1 Every one is justified in using such force as may be reasonably necessary in order to prevent the commission of suicide, or the commission of an offence which would be likely to cause immediate and serious injury to the person or property of any one, or in order to prevent any act being done which he or she believes, on reasonable grounds, would, if committed, amount to suicide or to any such offence. 2 This section is subject to. 1908 No 32 s 72 :, on, byof the(2019 No 67).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"41","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Prevention of suicide or certain offences","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is justified in using such force as may be reasonably necessary in order to prevent the commission of suicide, or the commission of an offence which would be likely to cause immediate and serious injury to the person or property of any one, or in order to prevent any act being done which he or she believes, on reasonable grounds, would, if committed, amount to suicide or to any such offence."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"extref":{"#text":"section 37","@_href":"LMS167599","@_id":"LMS603886"},"#text":"of the End of Life Choice Act 2019","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"This section is subject to."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 72","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 41(2)","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"6 November 2021","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 41","@_href":"LMS168876","@_id":"LMS603885"},"amending-leg":"End of Life Choice Act 2019","#text":":, on, byof the(2019 No 67).","@_id":"LMS603881"}}},"@_id":"DLM328259","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Preventing breach of the peace
sections.fullText: 42 Preventing breach of the peace 1 Every one who witnesses a breach of the peace is justified in interfering to prevent its continuance or renewal, and may detain any person committing it, in order to give him or her into the custody of a constable: provided that the person interfering shall use no more force than is reasonably necessary for preventing the continuance or renewal of the breach of the peace, or than is reasonably proportionate to the danger to be apprehended from its continuance or renewal. 2 Every constable who witnesses a breach of the peace, and every person lawfully assisting him or her, is justified in arresting any one whom he or she finds committing it. 3 Every constable is justified in receiving into custody any person given into his or her charge, as having been a party to a breach of the peace, by one who has witnessed it or whom the constable believes on reasonable and probable grounds to have witnessed it. 1908 No 32 s 66
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"42","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Preventing breach of the peace","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who witnesses a breach of the peace is justified in interfering to prevent its continuance or renewal, and may detain any person committing it, in order to give him or her into the custody of a constable:"},{"text":"provided that the person interfering shall use no more force than is reasonably necessary for preventing the continuance or renewal of the breach of the peace, or than is reasonably proportionate to the danger to be apprehended from its continuance or renewal."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every constable who witnesses a breach of the peace, and every person lawfully assisting him or her, is justified in arresting any one whom he or she finds committing it."}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every constable is justified in receiving into custody any person given into his or her charge, as having been a party to a breach of the peace, by one who has witnessed it or whom the constable believes on reasonable and probable grounds to have witnessed it."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 66","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328261","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Suppression of riot
sections.fullText: 43 Suppression of riot Every one is justified in using such force as is necessary to suppress a riot, if the force used is not disproportionate to the danger to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot. 1908 No 32 s 67
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"43","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Suppression of riot","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is justified in using such force as is necessary to suppress a riot, if the force used is not disproportionate to the danger to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 67","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328262","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Suppression of riot by Police
sections.fullText: 44 Suppression of riot by Police The seniorfor the time being acting at the place of any riot is justified in using and ordering to be used, and every constable is justified in using, such force as he or she believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary to suppress the riot, not being disproportionate to the danger which he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, is to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot. 1908 No 32 s 68 :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"44","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Suppression of riot by Police","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":"constable","#text":"The seniorfor the time being acting at the place of any riot is justified in using and ordering to be used, and every constable is justified in using, such force as he or she believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary to suppress the riot, not being disproportionate to the danger which he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, is to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 68","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 44","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 October 2008","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 116(a)﻿(ii)","@_href":"DLM1102349","@_id":"DLM2052911"},"amending-leg":"Policing Act 2008","#text":":, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72).","@_id":"DLM2052700"}}},"@_id":"DLM328263","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Suppression of riot by persons acting under lawful orders
sections.fullText: 45 Suppression of riot by persons acting under lawful orders 1 Every one, whether a member of the New Zealand forces or not, acting in good faith in obedience to orders for the suppression of any riot given by the seniorfor the time being acting at the place of the riot is justified in obeying the orders so given, unless those orders are manifestly unlawful; and is protected from criminal responsibility for using such force as he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary for carrying those orders into effect. 2 It is a question of law whether any particular order is manifestly unlawful or not. 1908 No 32 s 69 :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"45","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Suppression of riot by persons acting under lawful orders","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":"constable","#text":"Every one, whether a member of the New Zealand forces or not, acting in good faith in obedience to orders for the suppression of any riot given by the seniorfor the time being acting at the place of the riot is justified in obeying the orders so given, unless those orders are manifestly unlawful; and is protected from criminal responsibility for using such force as he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary for carrying those orders into effect."}}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"It is a question of law whether any particular order is manifestly unlawful or not."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 69","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 45(1)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 October 2008","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 116(a)﻿(ii)","@_href":"DLM1102349","@_id":"DLM2052912"},"amending-leg":"Policing Act 2008","#text":":, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72).","@_id":"DLM2052701"}}},"@_id":"DLM328264","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Suppression of riot by persons without orders
sections.fullText: 46 Suppression of riot by persons without orders Every one, whether a member of the New Zealand forces or not, who believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, that serious mischief will arise from a riot before there is time to procure the intervention of the Police, is justified in using such force as he or she believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary to suppress the riot, not being disproportionate to the danger which he or she believes, on reasonable grounds, is to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot. 1908 No 32 s 70
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"46","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Suppression of riot by persons without orders","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one, whether a member of the New Zealand forces or not, who believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, that serious mischief will arise from a riot before there is time to procure the intervention of the Police, is justified in using such force as he or she believes, in good faith and on reasonable and probable grounds, to be necessary to suppress the riot, not being disproportionate to the danger which he or she believes, on reasonable grounds, is to be apprehended from the continuance of the riot."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 70","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328265","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Protection of members of New Zealand forces
sections.fullText: 47 Protection of members of New Zealand forces 1 Every one bound as a member of the New Zealand forces to obey the lawful command of his or her superior officer is justified in obeying any command given him or her by any such officer for the suppression of a riot, unless the command is manifestly unlawful. 2 It is a question of law whether any particular command is manifestly unlawful or not. 1908 No 32 s 71
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"47","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Protection of members of New Zealand forces","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one bound as a member of the New Zealand forces to obey the lawful command of his or her superior officer is justified in obeying any command given him or her by any such officer for the suppression of a riot, unless the command is manifestly unlawful."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"It is a question of law whether any particular command is manifestly unlawful or not."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 71","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328266","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Self-defence and defence of another
sections.fullText: 48 Self-defence and defence of another 1 Every one is justified in using, in the defence of himself or herself or another, such force as, in the circumstances as he or she believes them to be, it is reasonable to use. 2 This section is subject to. :, on, by section 2(1) of the(1980 No 63). :, on, byof the(2019 No 67).

sections.fullText: 49 Self-defence against provoked assault :, on, by section 2(1) of the(1980 No 63).

sections.fullText: 50 Provocation defined :, on, by section 2(1) of the(1980 No 63).

sections.heading: Defence of person under protection
sections.fullText: 51 Defence of person under protection :, on, by section 2(1) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"51","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of person under protection","prov.body":"","notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 51","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(1) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM1882049"}}},"@_deletion-status":"repealed","@_id":"DLM328274","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Defence of movable property against trespasser
sections.fullText: 52 Defence of movable property against trespasser 1 Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing, and every one lawfully assisting him or her, is justified in using reasonable force to resist the taking of the thing by any trespasser or to retake it from any trespasser, if in either case he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to the trespasser. 2 1908 No 32 s 77 :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"52","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of movable property against trespasser","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing, and every one lawfully assisting him or her, is justified in using reasonable force to resist the taking of the thing by any trespasser or to retake it from any trespasser, if in either case he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to the trespasser."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 77","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 52(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM328278"}}},"@_id":"DLM328277","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Defence of movable property with claim of right
sections.fullText: 53 Defence of movable property with claim of right 1 Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing under a claim of right, and every one acting under his or her authority, is protected from criminal responsibility for defending his or her possession by the use of reasonable force, even against a person entitled by law to possession, if he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to the other person. 2 1908 No 32 s 78 :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"53","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of movable property with claim of right","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing under a claim of right, and every one acting under his or her authority, is protected from criminal responsibility for defending his or her possession by the use of reasonable force, even against a person entitled by law to possession, if he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to the other person."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 78","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 53(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM328280"}}},"@_id":"DLM328279","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Defence of movable property without claim of right
sections.fullText: 54 Defence of movable property without claim of right 1 Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing, but neither claiming right thereto nor acting under the authority of a person claiming right thereto, is neither justified in nor protected from criminal responsibility for defending his or her possession against a person entitled by law to possession. 2 1908 No 32 s 79 :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"54","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of movable property without claim of right","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one in peaceable possession of any movable thing, but neither claiming right thereto nor acting under the authority of a person claiming right thereto, is neither justified in nor protected from criminal responsibility for defending his or her possession against a person entitled by law to possession."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 79","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 54(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM328282"}}},"@_id":"DLM328281","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Defence of dwellinghouse
sections.fullText: 55 Defence of dwellinghouse Every one in peaceable possession of a dwellinghouse, and every one lawfully assisting him or her or acting by his or her authority, is justified in using such force as is necessary to prevent the forcible breaking and entering of the dwellinghouse by any person if he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, that there is no lawful justification for the breaking and entering. ;
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"55","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of dwellinghouse","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one in peaceable possession of a dwellinghouse, and every one lawfully assisting him or her or acting by his or her authority, is justified in using such force as is necessary to prevent the forcible breaking and entering of the dwellinghouse by any person if he or she believes, on reasonable and probable grounds, that there is no lawful justification for the breaking and entering."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":[{"#text":"1908 No 32 ss 80, 81","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},{"#text":"Criminal Code (1954) s 40 (Canada)","@_jurisdiction":"can"}],"#text":";"},"@_id":"DLM328283","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Defence of land or building
sections.fullText: 56 Defence of land or building 1 Every one in peaceable possession of any land or building, and every one lawfully assisting him or her or acting by his or her authority, is justified in using reasonable force to prevent any person from trespassing on the land or building or to remove him or her therefrom, if he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to that person. 2 1908 No 32 s 82 :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"56","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Defence of land or building","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one in peaceable possession of any land or building, and every one lawfully assisting him or her or acting by his or her authority, is justified in using reasonable force to prevent any person from trespassing on the land or building or to remove him or her therefrom, if he or she does not strike or do bodily harm to that person."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 82","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 56(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM328285"}}},"@_id":"DLM328284","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Assertion of right to land or building
sections.fullText: 57 Assertion of right to land or building 1 Every one is justified in peaceably entering in the daytime on any land or building to the possession of which he or she, or some person under whose authority he or she acts, is lawfully entitled, for the purpose of taking possession thereof. 2 3 1908 No 32 s 83 :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63). :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"57","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Assertion of right to land or building","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is justified in peaceably entering in the daytime on any land or building to the possession of which he or she, or some person under whose authority he or she acts, is lawfully entitled, for the purpose of taking possession thereof."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 83","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 57(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM328288"},{"amended-provision":"Section 57(3)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"1 January 1981","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1980","#text":":, on, by section 2(2) of the(1980 No 63).","@_id":"DLM1882050"}]}},"@_id":"DLM328287","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Exercise of right of way, etc
sections.fullText: 58 Exercise of right of way, etc Every one lawfully entitled to enter on any land for the exercise of any right of way or other easement or profit is justified in peaceably entering on the land for the purpose of exercising that right of way, easement, or profit: provided that if any one so entering has notice that his or her right to use that way or easement, or to take that profit, is disputed by the person in possession of the land, an assault committed by that person, or by any person acting under his or her authority, for the purpose of making the person entering desist from entry, shall be deemed to be provoked by the person entering. 1908 No 32 s 84
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"58","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Exercise of right of way, etc","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one lawfully entitled to enter on any land for the exercise of any right of way or other easement or profit is justified in peaceably entering on the land for the purpose of exercising that right of way, easement, or profit:"},{"text":"provided that if any one so entering has notice that his or her right to use that way or easement, or to take that profit, is disputed by the person in possession of the land, an assault committed by that person, or by any person acting under his or her authority, for the purpose of making the person entering desist from entry, shall be deemed to be provoked by the person entering."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 84","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328289","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Parental control
sections.fullText: 59 Parental control 1 Every parent of a child and every person in the place of a parent of the child is justified in using force if the force used is reasonable in the circumstances and is for the purpose of— a preventing or minimising harm to the child or another person; or b preventing the child from engaging or continuing to engage in conduct that amounts to a criminal offence; or c preventing the child from engaging or continuing to engage in offensive or disruptive behaviour; or d performing the normal daily tasks that are incidental to good care and parenting. 2 Nothing in subsection (1) or in any rule of common law justifies the use of force for the purpose of correction. 3 Subsection (2) prevails over subsection (1). 4 To avoid doubt, it is affirmed that the Police have the discretion not to prosecute complaints against a parent of a child or person in the place of a parent of a child in relation to an offence involving the use of force against a child, where the offence is considered to be so inconsequential that there is no public interest in proceeding with a prosecution. :, on, byof the(2007 No 18).

or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"preventing the child from engaging or continuing to engage in conduct that amounts to a criminal offence; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"preventing the child from engaging or continuing to engage in offensive or disruptive behaviour; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"d","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"performing the normal daily tasks that are incidental to good care and parenting."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Nothing in subsection (1) or in any rule of common law justifies the use of force for the purpose of correction."}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Subsection (2) prevails over subsection (1)."}]},{"label":{"#text":"4","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"To avoid doubt, it is affirmed that the Police have the discretion not to prosecute complaints against a parent of a child or person in the place of a parent of a child in relation to an offence involving the use of force against a child, where the offence is considered to be so inconsequential that there is no public interest in proceeding with a prosecution."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 59","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"21 June 2007","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 5","@_href":"DLM407671","@_id":"DLM822416"},"amending-leg":"Crimes (Substituted Section 59) Amendment Act 2007","#text":":, on, byof the(2007 No 18).","@_id":"DLM328294"}}},"@_id":"DLM328291","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Discipline on ship or aircraft
sections.fullText: 60 Discipline on ship or aircraft 1 The master or officer in command of a ship on a voyage or the pilot in command of an aircraft on a flight is justified in using and ordering the use of force for the purpose of maintaining good order and discipline on board his or her ship or aircraft if he or she believes on reasonable grounds that the use of force is necessary, and if the force used is reasonable in the circumstances. 2 Every one acting in good faith is justified in using force in obedience to any order given by the master or officer or pilot in command for the purpose aforesaid, if the force used is reasonable in the circumstances. 3 The reasonableness of the grounds on which the use of force was believed to be necessary, and the reasonableness of the force used, are questions of fact. 1908 No 32 s 85(2), (3)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"60","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Discipline on ship or aircraft","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"The master or officer in command of a ship on a voyage or the pilot in command of an aircraft on a flight is justified in using and ordering the use of force for the purpose of maintaining good order and discipline on board his or her ship or aircraft if he or she believes on reasonable grounds that the use of force is necessary, and if the force used is reasonable in the circumstances."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one acting in good faith is justified in using force in obedience to any order given by the master or officer or pilot in command for the purpose aforesaid, if the force used is reasonable in the circumstances."}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"The reasonableness of the grounds on which the use of force was believed to be necessary, and the reasonableness of the force used, are questions of fact."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 85(2), (3)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328295","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Surgical operations
sections.fullText: 61 Surgical operations Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for performing with reasonable care and skill any surgical operation upon any person for his or her benefit, if the performance of the operation was reasonable, having regard to the patient's state at the time and to all the circumstances of the case. 1908 No 32 s 86(1)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"61","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Surgical operations","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for performing with reasonable care and skill any surgical operation upon any person for his or her benefit, if the performance of the operation was reasonable, having regard to the patient's state at the time and to all the circumstances of the case."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 86(1)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328297","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Further provisions relating to surgical operations
sections.fullText: 61A Further provisions relating to surgical operations 1 Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for performing with reasonable care and skill any surgical operation upon any person if the operation is performed with the consent of that person, or of any person lawfully entitled to consent on his or her behalf to the operation, and for a lawful purpose. 2 Without limiting the termin subsection (1), a surgical operation that is performed for the purpose of rendering the patient sterile is performed for a lawful purpose. :, on, by section 2 of the(1977 No 113).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"61A","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Further provisions relating to surgical operations","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for performing with reasonable care and skill any surgical operation upon any person if the operation is performed with the consent of that person, or of any person lawfully entitled to consent on his or her behalf to the operation, and for a lawful purpose."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"def-term":{"#text":"lawful purpose","@_id":"DLM2170000"},"#text":"Without limiting the termin subsection (1), a surgical operation that is performed for the purpose of rendering the patient sterile is performed for a lawful purpose."}}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 61A","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"16 December 1977","amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 1977","#text":":, on, by section 2 of the(1977 No 113).","@_id":"DLM328299"}}},"@_id":"DLM328298","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Excess of force
sections.fullText: 62 Excess of force Every one authorised by law to use force is criminally responsible for any excess, according to the nature and quality of the act that constitutes the excess. 1908 No 32 s 86(2)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"62","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Excess of force","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one authorised by law to use force is criminally responsible for any excess, according to the nature and quality of the act that constitutes the excess."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 86(2)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328501","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Consent to death
sections.fullText: 63 Consent to death No one has a right to consent to the infliction of death upon himself or herself; and, if any person is killed, the fact that he or she gave any such consent shall not affect the criminal responsibility of any person who is a party to the killing. 1908 No 32 s 87
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"63","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Consent to death","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"No one has a right to consent to the infliction of death upon himself or herself; and, if any person is killed, the fact that he or she gave any such consent shall not affect the criminal responsibility of any person who is a party to the killing."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 87","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328502","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Obedience to de facto law
sections.fullText: 64 Obedience to de facto law Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for any act done in obedience to the laws for the time being made and enforced by those in possession de facto of the sovereign power in and over the place where the act is done. 1908 No 32 s 88
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"64","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Obedience to de facto law","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is protected from criminal responsibility for any act done in obedience to the laws for the time being made and enforced by those in possession de facto of the sovereign power in and over the place where the act is done."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 88","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328503","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Other enactments not affected
sections.fullText: 65 Other enactments not affected Nothing in this Act shall take away or diminish any authority given by any other enactment to arrest, detain, or put any restraint on any person. 1908 No 32 s 59
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"65","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Other enactments not affected","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Nothing in this Act shall take away or diminish any authority given by any other enactment to arrest, detain, or put any restraint on any person."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 59","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328504","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Parties to offences
sections.fullText: 66 Parties to offences 1 Every one is a party to and guilty of an offence who— a actually commits the offence; or b does or omits an act for the purpose of aiding any person to commit the offence; or c abets any person in the commission of the offence; or d incites, counsels, or procures any person to commit the offence. 2 Where 2 or more persons form a common intention to prosecute any unlawful purpose, and to assist each other therein, each of them is a party to every offence committed by any one of them in the prosecution of the common purpose if the commission of that offence was known to be a probable consequence of the prosecution of the common purpose. 1908 No 32 s 90
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"66","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Parties to offences","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is a party to and guilty of an offence who—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"actually commits the offence; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"does or omits an act for the purpose of aiding any person to commit the offence; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"abets any person in the commission of the offence; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"d","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"incites, counsels, or procures any person to commit the offence."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Where 2 or more persons form a common intention to prosecute any unlawful purpose, and to assist each other therein, each of them is a party to every offence committed by any one of them in the prosecution of the common purpose if the commission of that offence was known to be a probable consequence of the prosecution of the common purpose."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 90","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328506","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Conspiracy between spouses or civil union partners
sections.fullText: 67 Conspiracy between spouses or civil union partners A person is capable of conspiring with his or her spouse or civil union partner or with his or her spouse or civil union partner and any other person. :, on, byof the(2005 No 3).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"67","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Conspiracy between spouses or civil union partners","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"A person is capable of conspiring with his or her spouse or civil union partner or with his or her spouse or civil union partner and any other person."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 67","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"26 April 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM333795","@_id":"DLM822418"},"amending-leg":"Relationships (Statutory References) Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 3).","@_id":"DLM328508"}}},"@_id":"DLM328507","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Party to murder outside New Zealand
sections.fullText: 68 Party to murder outside New Zealand 1 Except as hereinafter provided, every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, in New Zealand, aids, incites, counsels, or procures the doing or omission of an act outside New Zealand which, if done or omitted in New Zealand, would be murder. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who, in New Zealand, incites, counsels, or attempts to procure the doing or omission of an act outside New Zealand which, if done or omitted in New Zealand, would be murder, when no such act is in fact done or omitted. 3 It is a defence to a charge under this section to prove that the doing or omission of the act was not an offence under the law of the place where it was, or was to be, done or omitted. 4 Nothing in this section limits or affects. 1908 No 32 s 189(b) :, on, byof the(2000 No 26).

sections.heading: Party to any other crime outside New Zealand
sections.fullText: 69 Party to any other crime outside New Zealand 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, in New Zealand, aids, incites, counsels, or procures the doing or omission outside New Zealand, by any person not owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand, of any act which, if done or omitted outside New Zealand by a person owing such allegiance, would be any of the crimes of treason, inciting to mutiny, or, as specified in. 1A A person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand commits an offence if the person, outside New Zealand, aids, incites, counsels, or procures the doing or omission outside New Zealand, by any person not owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand, of any act which, if done or omitted outside New Zealand by a person owing such allegiance, would be any of the crimes mentioned in subsection (1). 1B A person who commits an offence against subsection (1A) is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who, in New Zealand, incites, counsels, or attempts to procure the doing or omission outside New Zealand of any act which, if done or omitted in New Zealand, would be, when that act is not in fact done or omitted. 2A A person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand commits an offence if the person, outside New Zealand, incites, counsels, or attempts to procure the doing or omission outside New Zealand of any act which, if done or omitted in New Zealand, would be any of the crimes mentioned in subsection (1), when that act is not in fact done or omitted. 2B A person who commits an offence against subsection (2A) is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 3 Every one who, in New Zealand, aids, incites, counsels, or procures the doing or omission outside New Zealand of any act (not being an act to which the foregoing provisions of this section apply) which, if done or omitted in New Zealand, would be, is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding that prescribed for, or 7 years, whichever is the less: provided that it shall be a defence to a charge under this subsection to prove that the doing or omission of the act to which the charge relates was not an offence under the law of the place where it was, or was to be, done or omitted. 4 Except as otherwise provided in this Act, no one shall be convicted of inciting, counselling, or attempting to procure in New Zealand the doing or omission of an act outside New Zealand when that act is not in fact done or omitted. 5 Nothing in this section limits or affects. :, on, by section 2(2) of the(1982 No 157). :, on, byof the(2025 No 71). :, on, byof sections.heading: Party to certain acts or omissions in New Zealand
sections.fullText: 69A Party to certain acts or omissions in New Zealand Relevant act done or omitted Relevant act not done or omitted Interpretation 1 A person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand commits an offence if— a the person, in or outside New Zealand, aids, abets, incites, counsels, or procures a person who does not owe such allegiance to do or omit a relevant act in New Zealand; and b the relevant act is in fact done or omitted. 2 A person who commits an offence against subsection (1) is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 3 A person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand commits an offence if— a the person, in or outside New Zealand, incites, counsels, or attempts to procure a person who does not owe such allegiance to do or omit a relevant act in New Zealand; and b the relevant act is not in fact done or omitted. 4 A person who commits an offence against subsection (3) is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 5 In this section,means an act which, if done or omitted in New Zealand by a person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand, would be an offence against— a (treason); or b (inciting to mutiny); or c (espionage). :, on, byof the(2025 No 71).

sections.heading: Offence committed other than offence intended
sections.fullText: 70 Offence committed other than offence intended 1 Every one who incites, counsels, or procures another to be a party to an offence of which that other is afterwards guilty is a party to that offence, although it may be committed in a way different from that which was incited, counselled, or suggested. 2 Every one who incites, counsels, or procures another to be a party to an offence is a party to every offence which that other commits in consequence of such inciting, counselling, or procuring, and which the first-mentioned person knew to be likely to be committed in consequence thereof. 1908 No 32 s 91

sections.heading: Accessory after the fact
sections.fullText: 71 Accessory after the fact 1 An accessory after the fact to an offence is one who, knowing any person to have been a party to the offence, receives, comforts, or assists that person or tampers with or actively suppresses any evidence against him or her, in order to enable him or her to escape after arrest or to avoid arrest or conviction. 2 ; :, on, byof the(2019 No 4).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"71","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Accessory after the fact","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"An accessory after the fact to an offence is one who, knowing any person to have been a party to the offence, receives, comforts, or assists that person or tampers with or actively suppresses any evidence against him or her, in order to enable him or her to escape after arrest or to avoid arrest or conviction."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":""}],"@_deletion-status":"repealed"}]},"cf":{"citation":[{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 92","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},{"#text":"Criminal Code (1954) s 23 (Canada)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}],"#text":";"},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 71(2)","amending-operation":"repealed","amendment-date":"12 March 2019","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 4","@_href":"LMS22749","@_id":"LMS170479"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2019","#text":":, on, byof the(2019 No 4).","@_id":"LMS170463"}}},"@_id":"DLM328515","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Attempts
sections.fullText: 72 Attempts 1 Every one who, having an intent to commit an offence, does or omits an act for the purpose of accomplishing his or her object, is guilty of an attempt to commit the offence intended, whether in the circumstances it was possible to commit the offence or not. 2 The question whether an act done or omitted with intent to commit an offence is or is not only preparation for the commission of that offence, and too remote to constitute an attempt to commit it, is a question of law. 3 An act done or omitted with intent to commit an offence may constitute an attempt if it is immediately or proximately connected with the intended offence, whether or not there was any act unequivocally showing the intent to commit that offence. 1908 No 32 s 93

sections.heading: Treason
sections.fullText: 73 Treason Every one owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand commits treason who, within or outside New Zealand,— a kills or wounds or does grievous bodily harm to the Sovereign, or imprisons or restrains her or him; or b levies war against New Zealand; or c assists an enemy at war with New Zealand, or any armed forces against which New Zealand forces are engaged in hostilities, whether or not a state of war exists between New Zealand and any other country; or d incites or assists any person with force to invade New Zealand; or e uses force for the purpose of overthrowing the Government of New Zealand; or f conspires with any person to do anything mentioned in this section. ;

sections.heading: Punishment for treason or attempted treason
sections.fullText: 74 Punishment for treason or attempted treason 1 Except as otherwise provided in this Act, every one who commits treason within the meaning of any of the provisions of paragraphs (a) to (e) ofshall upon conviction be sentenced to. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who commits treason within the meaning of paragraph (f) of the said. 3 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand, attempts to commit treason within or outside New Zealand. 1908 No 32 ss 95, 99 :, on, byof the(1989 No 119).

sections.heading: Evidence of treason
sections.fullText: 75 Evidence of treason 1 No one shall be convicted of treason on the evidence of 1 witness only, unless the evidence of that witness is corroborated in some material particular by evidence implicating the. 2 Nothing in subsection (1) shall apply to treason within the meaning of paragraph (a) of. ; :, on, byof the(2011 No 85).

sections.heading: Punishment for being party to treason
sections.fullText: 76 Punishment for being party to treason Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who— a becomes an accessory after the fact to treason; or b knowing that a person is about to commit treason, fails without reasonable excuse to inform a constable as soon as possible or to use other reasonable efforts to prevent its commission. 1908 No 32 s 98

sections.heading: Inciting to mutiny
sections.fullText: 77 Inciting to mutiny Every one owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who, within or outside New Zealand, for any traitorous or mutinous purpose,— a endeavours at any time to seduce any person serving in the New Zealand forces from his or her duty; or b during any war or state of hostilities in which New Zealand forces are engaged, endeavours to seduce any person serving in any allied armed force, whether a Commonwealth force or not, from his or her duty. ;
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"77","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Inciting to mutiny","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one owing allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who, within or outside New Zealand, for any traitorous or mutinous purpose,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"endeavours at any time to seduce any person serving in the New Zealand forces from his or her duty; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"during any war or state of hostilities in which New Zealand forces are engaged, endeavours to seduce any person serving in any allied armed force, whether a Commonwealth force or not, from his or her duty."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":[{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 100","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},{"#text":"1954 No 29 s 3","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}],"#text":";"},"@_id":"DLM328525","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Espionage
sections.fullText: 78 Espionage 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, being a person who owes allegiance to the Sovereign in right of New Zealand, within or outside New Zealand,— if the communication or delivery or intended communication or intended delivery under paragraph (a) or paragraph (b) is likely to prejudice the security or defence of New Zealand. a with intent to prejudice the security or defence of New Zealand, communicates information or delivers any object to a country or organisation outside New Zealand or to a person acting on behalf of any such country or organisation; or b with intent to prejudice the security or defence of New Zealand and with the intention of communicating information or delivering any object to a country or organisation outside New Zealand or to a person acting on behalf of any such country or organisation,— i collects or records any information; or ii copies any document; or iii obtains any object; or iv makes any sketch, plan, model, or note; or v takes any photograph; or vi records any sound or image; or vii delivers any object to any person,— 2 In this section,— has the meaning given in includes information about military tactics, techniques, or procedures. :, on, by section 2(1) of the(1982 No 157). :, on, byof the(2025 No 71).

sections.heading: Improper conduct for or on behalf of foreign power to compromise protected New Zealand interest
sections.fullText: 78AAA Improper conduct for or on behalf of foreign power to compromise protected New Zealand interest Intentional conduct Reckless conduct Interpretation 1 A person commits an offence if— a the person engages in improper conduct for or on behalf of a foreign power; and b the person knows, or ought to know, that they are engaging in the conduct for or on behalf of a foreign power; and c the person engages in the conduct intending to compromise a protected New Zealand interest. 2 A person who commits an offence against subsection (1) is liable on conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 3 A person commits an offence if— a the person engages in improper conduct for or on behalf of a foreign power; and b the person knows, or ought to know, that they are engaging in the conduct for or on behalf of a foreign power; and c the person engages in the conduct being reckless as to whether doing so is likely to compromise a protected New Zealand interest. 4 A person who commits an offence against subsection (3) is liable on conviction to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 5 To avoid doubt,— a the fact that a person protects information for a lawful purpose in the ordinary course of business, a profession, or an occupation, whether paid or unpaid, is not, by itself, a sufficient basis for inferring that the person is engaging in improper conduct: b the fact that a person engages in any protest, advocacy, or dissent, or engages in any strike, lockout, or other industrial action, is not, by itself, a sufficient basis for inferring that the person is— i intending to compromise a protected New Zealand interest; or ii reckless as to whether the person’s conduct is likely to compromise a protected New Zealand interest. 6 Examples of conduct contemplated by subsection (5) (a) include the protection of trade secrets, commercially sensitive information, personal information, journalists’ sources, or legally privileged communications, including by way of encryption. 7 In this section, conduct that a person engages in— a includes conduct that is— i instigated, directed, or supervised by a foreign power; or ii undertaken in collaboration with, or with the assistance, funding, or agreement of, a foreign power; but b excludes conduct that constitutes the lawful performance of the person’s functions or duties as an employee, contractor, authorised representative, or agent of the Government of New Zealand. 8 In this section,— means any of the following: a a foreign government (as defined in): b a foreign public agency (as defined in): c a foreign public enterprise (as defined): d a foreign public official (as defined in, but as if the references to a public international organisation were removed): e a political party within a foreign country (as defined in) that constitutes, forms part of, or represents a foreign government (as defined in section 105C): f a person in their capacity as a holder of a political or official post if— i the person holds that post as a result of, or in the course of, their membership of a political party referred to in paragraph (e); or ii the person is subject to the direction or control of, or is significantly influenced by, a political party referred to in paragraph (e) in exercising the functions of that post: g a company, body, organisation, or association whose main purpose is to support a political party referred to in paragraph (e) means conduct that includes any of the following: a conduct that is of a covert nature, which may include— i concealing a person’s actions or identity: ii obscuring the existence of an association or relationship: iii collecting or sharing information about a person without their knowledge or consent: b conduct that is of a deceptive nature, which may include— i misrepresenting or obfuscating a person’s purpose in acting or not acting: ii failing to disclose the consequences of acting or not acting in accordance with any representation: iii making a false representation: iv omitting any material particular in dealing with another person: c conduct that is of a coercive nature, which may include— i using intimidating, threatening, manipulative, or exploitative behaviour against another person: ii enabling the denial or restriction of access to property or services that another person would otherwise be entitled to access: d conduct that is of a corruptive nature, which may include abusing a person’s position or authority in a manner that enables the person to— i exert undue influence or control over another person: ii make a monetary or personal gain means any of the following: a the security or defence of New Zealand: b the economic well-being of New Zealand: c the international relations of New Zealand: d the functioning of the executive, judiciary, or legislature of New Zealand: e the exercise or performance of any public function, duty, or power conferred or imposed on a person or body by or pursuant to the law of New Zealand: f the fair and impartial conduct of any election, poll, or referendum under the law of New Zealand: g the ability of any person to exercise in New Zealand any human, civil, political, or democratic right recognised under the law of New Zealand: h the safety or security of— i any person in New Zealand; or ii any citizen of New Zealand, whether or not that person is in New Zealand; or iii any person ordinarily resident in New Zealand, whether or not that person is in New Zealand. :, on, byof the(2025 No 71).
