sections.heading: Punishment of perjury
sections.fullText: 109 Punishment of perjury 1 Except as provided in subsection (2), every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who commits perjury. 2 If perjury is committed in order to procure the conviction of a personfor any offence for which the maximum punishment is not less than 3 years' imprisonment, the punishment may be imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 1908 No 32 s 131 :, on, byof the(1989 No 119).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"109","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Punishment of perjury","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Except as provided in subsection (2), every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who commits perjury."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"struckoutwords":"","#text":"If perjury is committed in order to procure the conviction of a personfor any offence for which the maximum punishment is not less than 3 years' imprisonment, the punishment may be imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 131","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 109(2)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"26 December 1989","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 3(3)","@_href":"DLM193579","@_id":"DLM822775"},"amending-leg":"Abolition of the Death Penalty Act 1989","#text":":, on, byof the(1989 No 119).","@_id":"DLM328797"}}},"@_id":"DLM328796","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: False oaths
sections.fullText: 110 False oaths Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who, being required or authorised by law to make any statement on oath or affirmation, thereupon makes a statement that would amount to perjury if made in a judicial proceeding. 1908 No 32 s 132
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"110","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"False oaths","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who, being required or authorised by law to make any statement on oath or affirmation, thereupon makes a statement that would amount to perjury if made in a judicial proceeding."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 132","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328798","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: False statements or declarations
sections.fullText: 111 False statements or declarations Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, on any occasion on which he is required or permitted by law to make any statement or declaration before any officer or person authorised by law to take or receive it, or before any notary public to be certified by him as such notary, makes a statement or declaration that would amount to perjury if made on oath in a judicial proceeding. 1908 No 32 s 133
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"111","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"False statements or declarations","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, on any occasion on which he is required or permitted by law to make any statement or declaration before any officer or person authorised by law to take or receive it, or before any notary public to be certified by him as such notary, makes a statement or declaration that would amount to perjury if made on oath in a judicial proceeding."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 133","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM328799","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Evidence of perjury, false oath, or false statement
sections.fullText: 112 Evidence of perjury, false oath, or false statement No one shall be convicted of perjury, or of any offence against, on the evidence of 1 witness only, unless the evidence of that witness is corroborated in some material particular by evidence implicating the. ; Criminal Code (1954) s 115 (Canada) :, on, byof the(2011 No 85).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"112","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Evidence of perjury, false oath, or false statement","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":[{"#text":"section 110","@_href":"DLM328798","@_id":"DLM822776"},{"#text":"section 111","@_href":"DLM328799","@_id":"DLM822777"}],"#text":"or","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"insertwords":"defendant","#text":"No one shall be convicted of perjury, or of any offence against, on the evidence of 1 witness only, unless the evidence of that witness is corroborated in some material particular by evidence implicating the."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 134","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"; Criminal Code (1954) s 115 (Canada)"},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 112","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 July 2013","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 6","@_href":"DLM4057909","@_id":"DLM5348231"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 4) 2011","#text":":, on, byof the(2011 No 85).","@_id":"DLM5347946"}}},"@_id":"DLM329000","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Fabricating evidence
sections.fullText: 113 Fabricating evidence Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, with intent to mislead any tribunal holding any judicial proceeding to whichapplies, fabricates evidence by any means other than perjury. 1908 No 32 s 135
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"113","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Fabricating evidence","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 108","@_href":"DLM328793","@_id":"DLM822778"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, with intent to mislead any tribunal holding any judicial proceeding to whichapplies, fabricates evidence by any means other than perjury."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 135","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329001","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Use of purported affidavit or declaration
sections.fullText: 114 Use of purported affidavit or declaration Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who— a signs a writing that purports to be an affidavit sworn before him or her or a statutory declaration taken by him or her, when the writing was not so sworn or taken, or when he or she knows that he or she has no authority to administer that oath or take that declaration; or b uses or offers for use any writing purporting to be an affidavit or statutory declaration that he or she knows was not sworn or made, as the case may be, by the deponent or before a person authorised to administer that oath or take that declaration. Criminal Code (1954) s 118 (Canada)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"114","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Use of purported affidavit or declaration","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"signs a writing that purports to be an affidavit sworn before him or her or a statutory declaration taken by him or her, when the writing was not so sworn or taken, or when he or she knows that he or she has no authority to administer that oath or take that declaration; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"uses or offers for use any writing purporting to be an affidavit or statutory declaration that he or she knows was not sworn or made, as the case may be, by the deponent or before a person authorised to administer that oath or take that declaration."}]}]}]}]},"cf":"Criminal Code (1954) s 118 (Canada)","@_id":"DLM329002","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Conspiring to bring false accusation
sections.fullText: 115 Conspiring to bring false accusation Every one who conspires to prosecute any person for any alleged offence, knowing that person to be innocent thereof, is liable— a to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years if that person might, on conviction of the alleged offence, be sentencedto preventive detention, or to imprisonment for a term of 3 years or more: b to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years if that person might, on conviction of the alleged offence, be sentenced to imprisonment for a term less than 3 years. 1908 No 32 s 136 :, on, byof the(1989 No 119).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"115","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Conspiring to bring false accusation","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who conspires to prosecute any person for any alleged offence, knowing that person to be innocent thereof, is liable—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"struckoutwords":"","#text":"to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years if that person might, on conviction of the alleged offence, be sentencedto preventive detention, or to imprisonment for a term of 3 years or more:"}}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years if that person might, on conviction of the alleged offence, be sentenced to imprisonment for a term less than 3 years."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 136","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 115(a)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"26 December 1989","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 3(4)","@_href":"DLM193579","@_id":"DLM822779"},"amending-leg":"Abolition of the Death Penalty Act 1989","#text":":, on, byof the(1989 No 119).","@_id":"DLM329004"}}},"@_id":"DLM329003","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Conspiring to defeat justice
sections.fullText: 116 Conspiring to defeat justice Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who conspires to obstruct, prevent, pervert, or defeat the course of justice. 1908 No 32 s 137 :, on, byof the(2002 No 20).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"116","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Conspiring to defeat justice","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":"in New Zealand or the course of justice in an overseas jurisdiction","#text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who conspires to obstruct, prevent, pervert, or defeat the course of justice."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 137","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 116","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"18 June 2002","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 6(1)","@_href":"DLM145455","@_id":"DLM822780"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2002","#text":":, on, byof the(2002 No 20).","@_id":"DLM329006"}}},"@_id":"DLM329005","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Corrupting juries and witnesses
sections.fullText: 117 Corrupting juries and witnesses Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who— a dissuades or attempts to dissuade a person, by threats, bribes, or other corrupt means, from giving evidence in any cause or matter (whether civil or criminal, and whether tried or to be tried in New Zealand or in an overseas jurisdiction); or b influences or attempts to influence, by threats or bribes or other corrupt means, a member of a jury in his or her conduct as such (whether in a cause or matter tried or to be tried in New Zealand or in an overseas jurisdiction, and whether the member has been sworn as a member of a particular jury or not); or c accepts any bribe or other corrupt consideration to abstain from giving evidence (whether in a cause or matter tried or to be tried in New Zealand or in an overseas jurisdiction); or d accepts any bribe or other corrupt consideration on account of his or her conduct as a member of a jury (whether in a cause or matter tried or to be tried in New Zealand or in an overseas jurisdiction, and whether the member has been sworn as a member of a particular jury or not); or e wilfully attempts in any other way to obstruct, prevent, pervert, or defeat the course of justice in New Zealand or the course of justice in an overseas jurisdiction. :, on, byof the(2002 No 20).

or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"accepts any bribe or other corrupt consideration to abstain from giving evidence (whether in a cause or matter tried or to be tried in New Zealand or in an overseas jurisdiction); sections.heading: Assisting escape of prisoners of war or internees
sections.fullText: 118 Assisting escape of prisoners of war or internees Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who knowingly and wilfully— a assists any prisoner of war detained in New Zealand, or any person interned in New Zealand, to escape from any place in which he or she is for the time being detained; or b assists any such prisoner or person as aforesaid, suffered to be at large on his or her parole in New Zealand, to escape from the place where he or she is at large on his or her parole. 1908 No 32 s 140

sections.heading: Breaking
sections.fullText: 119 Breaking Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who by force or violence breaks any, with intent to set at liberty himself or herself or any other person detained therein. ; :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50).

sections.heading: Escape from lawful custody
sections.fullText: 120 Escape from lawful custody 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who,— a having been convicted of an offence, escapes from any lawful custody in which he or she may be under the conviction; or b whether convicted or not, escapes from anyin which he or she is lawfully detained; or ba being subject to an order or direction made under any ofor, escapes from the place in which he or she is required to stay under the order; or bb being subject to a public protection order made under the, escapes from the residence in which he or she is required to stay under that Act; or c being in lawful custody otherwise than aforesaid, escapes from such custody. 2 For the purposes of this section, custody under an illegal warrant or other irregular process shall be deemed to be lawful. ; :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2003 No 115). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2014 No 68).

sections.heading: Assisting escape from lawful custody
sections.fullText: 121 Assisting escape from lawful custody 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who— a rescues any person from lawful custody, whether in aor not; or b assists any person in escaping or attempting to escape from lawful custody, whether in aor not; or c with intent to facilitate the escape of any person lawfully detained in a, conveys or causes to be conveyed into anyany thing whatever. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who— a being a constable who has any person in his or her lawful custody, voluntarily and intentionally permits that person to escape from such custody: aa being a security officer (within the meaning of) in whose custody any person is lawfully detained, voluntarily and intentionally permits that person to escape from such custody: b being an officer of ain which any person is lawfully detained, voluntarily and intentionally permits that person to escape from the. 3 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year who, by failing to perform any legal duty, permits any person in his or her lawful custody to escape. 4 For the purposes of this section, custody under an irregular warrant or other irregular process shall be deemed to be lawful. ; :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, by section 27(1) of the(1994 No 120). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50).

sections.heading: Assisting escape of mentally impaired person detained for offence
sections.fullText: 122 Assisting escape of mentally impaired person detained for offence Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who— a rescues any person who is ordered to be detained as a special patient under theor as a special care recipient under thewhile that person is being taken to or from a hospital within the meaning of the Mental Health (Compulsory Assessment and Treatment) Act 1992, or a secure facility within the meaning of the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003, or any other place; or b being a constable, an officer of a, a security officer within the meaning of, or an officer of or employee in any hospital within the meaning of the, or a secure facility within the meaning of the, and who has in his or her custody any person lawfully detained as a special patient under the Mental Health (Compulsory Assessment and Treatment) Act 1992 or as a special care recipient under the Intellectual Disability (Compulsory Care and Rehabilitation) Act 2003, voluntarily and intentionally permits that person to escape from custody, whether while the person is being taken to or from any of the places specified in paragraph (a) or otherwise. :, on, byof the(2003 No 115). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50).

sections.heading: Distribution or exhibition of indecent matter
sections.fullText: 124 Distribution or exhibition of indecent matter 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who, without lawful justification or excuse,— a sells, exposes for sale, or otherwise distributes to the public any indecent model or object; or b exhibits or presents in or within view of any place to which the public have or are permitted to have access any indecent object or indecent show or performance c exhibits or presents in the presence of any person in consideration or expectation of any payment or otherwise for gain, any indecent show or performance. 2 It is a defence to a charge under this section to prove that the public good was served by the acts alleged to have been done. 3 It is a question of law whether the sale, exposure for sale, distribution, exhibition, or presentation might in the circumstances serve the public good, and whether there is evidence of excess beyond what the public good requires; but it is a question of fact whether or not the acts complained of did so serve the public good and whether or not there was such excess. 4 It is no defence that the person charged did not know that the model, object, show, or performance to which the charge relates was indecent, unless that person also satisfies the court— a that he or she had no reasonable opportunity of knowing it; and b that in the circumstances his or her ignorance was excusable. 5 No private prosecution (as defined in) for an offence against this section can be commenced without the Attorney-General's consent. 6 Nothing in this section shall apply to any publication within the meaning of the, whether the publication is objectionable within the meaning of that Act or not. 7 1908 No 32 s 157 :, on, by section 3(2) of the(1966 No 98). :, on, by section 3(1) of the(1966 No 98). :, on, byof the(2015 No 44). :, on, byof the(1993 No 94). :, on, byof the(1993 No 94).

sections.heading: Indecent communication with young person under 16
sections.fullText: 124A Indecent communication with young person under 16 1 A person of or over the age of 16 years is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years if he or she intentionally exposes a person under the age of 16 years (the) to indecent material (whether written, spoken, visual, or otherwise, alone or in combination) in communicating in any manner, directly or indirectly, with the young person. 2 A reference in subsection (1) to a person under the age of 16 years, or to the young person, includes a reference to a constable (as defined in) who pretends to be a person under the age of 16 years (the) if the person charged with an offence against subsection (1), when communicating with the fictitious young person and exposing the fictitious young person to indecent material, believed that the fictitious young person was a person under the age of 16 years. 3 It is a defence to a charge under subsection (1) if the person charged proves that,— a before communicating with the young person and exposing the young person to the indecent material, the person charged had taken reasonable steps to find out whether the young person was of or over the age of 16 years; and b at the time of communicating with the young person and exposing the young person to the indecent material, the person charged believed on reasonable grounds that the young person was of or over the age of 16 years. 4 It is no defence to a charge under subsection (1) that the person charged did not know that the material to which the charge relates was indecent, unless the person charged also proves— a that the person charged had no reasonable opportunity of knowing it; and b that in the circumstances the ignorance of the person charged was excusable. 5 No private prosecution (as defined in) for an offence against this section can be commenced without the Attorney-General's consent. Sexual Offences (Scotland) Act 2009, s 24 :, on, byof the(2015 No 44).

sections.heading: Indecent act in public place
sections.fullText: 125 Indecent act in public place 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who wilfully does any indecent act in any place to which the public have or are permitted to have access, or within view of any such place. 2 It is a defence to a charge under this section if the person charged proves that he or she had reasonable grounds for believing that he or she would not be observed. 3 For the purposes of this section, the termincludes any railway carriage, and also includes any ship, aircraft, or vehicle used for the carriage of passengers for hire or reward. 1908 No 32 s 156(a)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"125","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Indecent act in public place","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who wilfully does any indecent act in any place to which the public have or are permitted to have access, or within view of any such place."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"It is a defence to a charge under this section if the person charged proves that he or she had reasonable grounds for believing that he or she would not be observed."}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"def-term":{"#text":"place","@_id":"DLM329046"},"#text":"For the purposes of this section, the termincludes any railway carriage, and also includes any ship, aircraft, or vehicle used for the carriage of passengers for hire or reward."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 156(a)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329045","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Indecent act with intent to insult or offend
sections.fullText: 126 Indecent act with intent to insult or offend Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who with intent to insult or offend any person does any indecent act in any place. 1908 No 32 s 156(b)
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"126","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Indecent act with intent to insult or offend","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who with intent to insult or offend any person does any indecent act in any place."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 156(b)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329047","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: No presumption because of age
sections.fullText: 127 No presumption because of age There is no presumption of law that a person is incapable of sexual connection because of his or her age. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"127","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"No presumption because of age","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"There is no presumption of law that a person is incapable of sexual connection because of his or her age."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 127","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"20 May 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM346175","@_id":"DLM822945"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 41).","@_id":"DLM329050"}}},"@_id":"DLM329049","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Sexual violation defined
sections.fullText: 128 Sexual violation defined 1 Sexual violation is the act of a person who— a rapes another person; or b has unlawful sexual connection with another person. 2 Person A rapes person B if person A has sexual connection with person B, effected by the penetration of person B's genitalia by person A's penis,— a without person B's consent to the connection; and b without believing on reasonable grounds that person B consents to the connection. 3 Person A has unlawful sexual connection with person B if person A has sexual connection with person B— a without person B's consent to the connection; and b without believing on reasonable grounds that person B consents to the connection. 4 One person may be convicted of the sexual violation of another person at a time when they were married to each other. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"128","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Sexual violation defined","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Sexual violation is the act of a person who—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"rapes another person; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"has unlawful sexual connection with another person."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Person A rapes person B if person A has sexual connection with person B, effected by the penetration of person B's genitalia by person A's penis,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"without person B's consent to the connection; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"without believing on reasonable grounds that person B consents to the connection."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Person A has unlawful sexual connection with person B if person A has sexual connection with person B—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"without person B's consent to the connection; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"without believing on reasonable grounds that person B consents to the connection."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"4","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"One person may be convicted of the sexual violation of another person at a time when they were married to each other."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 128","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"20 May 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM346175","@_id":"DLM822950"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 41).","@_id":"DLM329056"}}},"@_id":"DLM329051","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Allowing sexual activity does not amount to consent in some circumstances
sections.fullText: 128A Allowing sexual activity does not amount to consent in some circumstances 1 A person does not consent to sexual activity just because he or she does not protest or offer physical resistance to the activity. 2 A person does not consent to sexual activity if he or she allows the activity because of— a force applied to him or her or some other person; or b the threat (express or implied) of the application of force to him or her or some other person; or c the fear of the application of force to him or her or some other person. 3 A person does not consent to sexual activity if the activity occurs while he or she is asleep or unconscious. 4 A person does not consent to sexual activity if the activity occurs while he or she is so affected by alcohol or some other drug that he or she cannot consent or refuse to consent to the activity. 5 A person does not consent to sexual activity if the activity occurs while he or she is affected by an intellectual, mental, or physical condition or impairment of such a nature and degree that he or she cannot consent or refuse to consent to the activity. 6 One person does not consent to sexual activity with another person if he or she allows the sexual activity because he or she is mistaken about who the other person is. 7 A person does not consent to an act of sexual activity if he or she allows the act because he or she is mistaken about its nature and quality. 8 This section does not limit the circumstances in which a person does not consent to sexual activity. 9 For the purposes of this section,— includes acquiesces in, submits to, participates in, and undertakes , in relation to a person, means— a sexual connection with the person; or b the doing on the person of an indecent act that, without the person's consent, would be an indecent assault of the person. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Sexual violation
sections.fullText: 128B Sexual violation 1 Every one who commits sexual violation is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 20 years. 2 A person convicted of sexual violation must be sentenced to imprisonment unless, having regard to the matters stated in subsection (3), the court thinks that the person should not be sentenced to imprisonment. 3 The matters are— a the particular circumstances of the person convicted; and b the particular circumstances of the offence, including the nature of the conduct constituting it. 4 This section does not apply if— a person B (as described in) is under the age of 12 years; or b it cannot be established whether person B was under or over the age of 12 years. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41). :, on, byof the(2025 No 36).

sections.heading: Attempted sexual violation and assault with intent to commit sexual violation
sections.fullText: 129 Attempted sexual violation and assault with intent to commit sexual violation 1 Every one who attempts to commit sexual violation is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 2 Every one who assaults another person with intent to commit sexual violation of the other person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"129","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Attempted sexual violation and assault with intent to commit sexual violation","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who attempts to commit sexual violation is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who assaults another person with intent to commit sexual violation of the other person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 129","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"20 May 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM346175","@_id":"DLM822975"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 41).","@_id":"DLM329074"}}},"@_id":"DLM329070","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Sexual conduct with consent induced by certain threats
sections.fullText: 129A Sexual conduct with consent induced by certain threats 1 Every one who has sexual connection with another person knowing that the other person has been induced to consent to the connection by threat is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 2 Every one who does an indecent act on another person knowing that the other person has been induced to consent to the act by threat is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years. 3 For the purposes of subsection (1), a person who has sexual connection with another person knows that the other person has been induced to consent to the sexual connection by threat if (and only if) he or she knows that the other person has been induced to consent to the sexual connection by an express or implied threat of a kind described in subsection (5). 4 For the purposes of subsection (2),— a a person who does an indecent act on another person knows that the other person has been induced to consent to the act by threat if (and only if) he or she knows that the other person has been induced to consent to the act by an express or implied threat of a kind described in subsection (5); and b a person is induced to consent to an indecent act whether— i he or she is induced to consent to the doing of an indecent act with or on him or her; or ii he or she is induced to consent to do an indecent act himself or herself. 5 The kinds of threat referred to in subsections (3) and (4) (a) are— a a threat that the person making the threat or some other person will commit an offence that— i is punishable by imprisonment; but ii does not involve the actual or threatened application of force to any person; and b a threat that the person making the threat or some other person will make an accusation or disclosure (whether true or false) about misconduct by any person (whether living or dead) that is likely to damage seriously the reputation of the person against or about whom the accusation or disclosure is made; and c a threat that the person making the threat will make improper use, to the detriment of the person consenting, of a power or authority arising out of— i an occupational or vocational position held by the person making the threat; or ii a commercial relationship existing between the person making the threat and the person consenting. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Incest
sections.fullText: 130 Incest 1 Sexual connection is incest if— a it is between 2 people whose relationship is that of parent and child, siblings, half-siblings, or grandparent and grandchild; and b the person charged knows of the relationship. 2 Every one of or over the age of 16 years who commits incest is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"130","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Incest","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Sexual connection is incest if—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"it is between 2 people whose relationship is that of parent and child, siblings, half-siblings, or grandparent and grandchild; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the person charged knows of the relationship."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one of or over the age of 16 years who commits incest is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 130","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"20 May 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM346175","@_id":"DLM1917593"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 41).","@_id":"DLM1886200"}}},"@_id":"DLM329081","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Sexual conduct with dependent family member
sections.fullText: 131 Sexual conduct with dependent family member 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who has sexual connection with a dependent family member under the age of 18 years. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who attempts to have sexual connection with a dependent family member under the age of 18 years. 3 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who does an indecent act on a dependent family member under the age of 18 years. 4 The dependent family member cannot be charged as a party to the offence. 5 It is not a defence to a charge under this section that the dependent family member consented. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Dependent family member defined
sections.fullText: 131A Dependent family member defined 1 For the purposes of, one person is a dependent family member of another person— a if the other person has power or authority over him or her, and is— i his or her parent, step-parent, foster parent, guardian, uncle, or aunt; or ii a parent, step-parent, or foster parent of a person described in subparagraph (i); or iii a child of his or her parent or step-parent; or iv the spouse or de facto partner of a person described in subparagraph (i) or subparagraph (ii) or subparagraph (iii); or b if they are members of the same family, whanau, or other culturally recognised family group, and the other person— i is not a person referred to in paragraph (a); but ii has a responsibility for, or significant role in, his or her care or upbringing; or c if he or she is living with the other person as a member of the other person's family, and the other person is not a person referred to in paragraph (a), but has— i power or authority over him or her; and ii a responsibility for, or significant role in, his or her care or upbringing. 2 In subsection (1),— , in relation to a person, includes a half-sister of one of the person's parents includes a former foster parent — a means guardian by virtue of theor the; and b includes a former guardian includes a former step-parent , in relation to a person, includes a half-brother of one of the person's parents. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41). paragraph (a):, on, byof the(2017 No 31).

sections.heading: Grooming for sexual conduct with young person
sections.fullText: 131AB Grooming for sexual conduct with young person 1 A person aged 18 years or over is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years, if— a they communicate by words or conduct with a person under the age of 16 years (the); and b they do so intending to facilitate the young person engaging or being involved in conduct that would be an offence against this Part, or against any of paragraphs (a) (i), (d) (i), (e) (i), or (f) (i) of. 2 It is immaterial whether or not a response is made to the communication by the young person. 3 A reference in this section to a young person under the age of 16 years or the young person includes a reference to a constable who pretends to be a young person under the age of 16 years (the) if the offender, when taking any of the actions described in subsection (1), believed that the fictitious young person was a young person under the age of 16 years. 4 It is a defence to a charge under subsection (1) if the person charged proves that,— a before the time they took the action concerned, they had taken reasonable steps to find out whether the young person was of or over the age of 16 years; and b at the time they took the action concerned, they believed on reasonable grounds that the young person was of or over the age of 16 years. :, on, byof the(2023 No 16).

sections.heading: Meeting young personfollowing sexual grooming, etc
sections.fullText: 131B Meeting young personfollowing sexual grooming, etc 1 Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years if,— a having met or communicated with a person under the age of 16 years (the) on an earlier occasion, he or she takes one of the following actions: i intentionally meets the young person: ii travels with the intention of meeting the young person: iii arranges for or persuades the young person to travel with the intention of meeting him or her; and b at the time of taking the action, he or she intends— i to take in respect of the young person an action that, if taken in New Zealand, would be an offence against this Part, or against any of paragraphs (a) (i), (d) (i), (e) (i), (f) (i), of; or ii that the young person should do on him or her an act the doing of which would, if he or she permitted it to be done in New Zealand, be an offence against this Part on his or her part. 1A A reference in this section to a young person under the age of 16 years or the young person includes a reference to a constable who pretends to be a young person under the age of 16 years (the) if the offender, when taking any of the actions described in subsection (1), believed that the fictitious young person was a young person under the age of 16 years. 2 It is a defence to a charge under subsection (1) if the person charged proves that,— a before the time he or she took the action concerned, he or she had taken reasonable steps to find out whether the young person was of or over the age of 16 years; and b at the time he or she took the action concerned, he or she believed on reasonable grounds that the young person was of or over the age of 16 years. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41). :, on, byof the(2011 No 79). :, on, byof the(2011 No 79).

sections.heading: Sexual conduct with child under 12
sections.fullText: 132 Sexual conduct with child under 12 1 Every one who has sexual connection with a child is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding. 2 Every one who attempts to have sexual connection with a child is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 3 Every one who does an indecent act on a child is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 4 It is not a defence to a charge under this section that the person charged believed that the child was of or over the age of 12 years. 5 It is not a defence to a charge under this section that the child consented. 6 In this section,— a — i means a person under the age of 12 years; and ii for the purposes of subsection (1), includes person B (as described in) if it cannot be established whether person B was under or over the age of 12 years; and b doing an indecent act on a child includes indecently assaulting the child. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41). :, on, byof the(2025 No 36). :, on, byof the(2025 No 36).

sections.heading: Sexual conduct with young person under 16
sections.fullText: 134 Sexual conduct with young person under 16 1 Every one who has sexual connection with a young person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 2 Every one who attempts to have sexual connection with a young person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 3 Every one who does an indecent act on a young person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years. 4 No person can be convicted of a charge under this section if he or she was married to the young person concerned at the time of the sexual connection or indecent act concerned. 5 The young person in respect of whom an offence against this section was committed cannot be charged as a party to the offence if the person who committed the offence was of or over the age of 16 years when the offence was committed. 6 In this section,— a means a person under the age of 16 years; and b doing an indecent act on a young person includes indecently assaulting the young person. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Defence to charge under section 134
sections.fullText: 134A Defence to charge under section 134 1 It is a defence to a charge underif the person charged proves that,— a before the time of the act concerned, he or she had taken reasonable steps to find out whether the young person concerned was of or over the age of 16 years; and b at the time of the act concerned, he or she believed on reasonable grounds that the young person was of or over the age of 16 years; and c the young person consented. 2 Except to the extent provided in subsection (1),— a it is not a defence to a charge underthat the young person concerned consented; and b it is not a defence to a charge underthat the person charged believed that the young person concerned was of or over the age of 16 years. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Indecent assault
sections.fullText: 135 Indecent assault Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who indecently assaults another person. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Sexual exploitation of person with significant impairment
sections.fullText: 138 Sexual exploitation of person with significant impairment 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who has exploitative sexual connection with a person with a significant impairment. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who attempts to have exploitative sexual connection with a person with a significant impairment. 3 For the purposes of subsections (1) and (2), a person has exploitative sexual connection with a person with a significant impairment (the) if he or she— a has sexual connection with the impaired person knowing that the impaired person is a person with a significant impairment; and b has obtained the impaired person's acquiescence in, submission to, participation in, or undertaking of the connection by taking advantage of the impairment. 4 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who exploitatively does an indecent act on a person with a significant impairment. 5 For the purposes of subsection (4), a person exploitatively does an indecent act on a person with a significant impairment (the) if he or she— a does an indecent act on the impaired person knowing that the impaired person is a person with a significant impairment; and b has obtained the impaired person's acquiescence in, submission to, participation in, or undertaking of the doing of the act by taking advantage of the impairment. 6 For the purposes of this section, ais an intellectual, mental, or physical condition or impairment (or a combination of 2 or more intellectual, mental, or physical conditions or impairments) that affects a person to such an extent that it significantly impairs the person's capacity— a to understand the nature of sexual conduct; or b to understand the nature of decisions about sexual conduct; or c to foresee the consequences of decisions about sexual conduct; or d to communicate decisions about sexual conduct. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Compelling indecent act with animal
sections.fullText: 142A Compelling indecent act with animal 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who compels any other person, by the actual or threatened application of force to that other person or some other person, to perform, or to submit to or acquiesce in, any act of indecency with an animal, whether or not involving penetration. 2 :, on, byof the(1985 No 160). :, on, byof the(2002 No 9).

sections.heading: Bestiality
sections.fullText: 143 Bestiality 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who commits bestiality. 2 This offence is complete upon penetration. ;

sections.heading: Indecency with animal
sections.fullText: 144 Indecency with animal Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who commits any act of indecency with an animal.
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"144","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Indecency with animal","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who commits any act of indecency with an animal."}]}]},"@_id":"DLM329261","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Sexual conduct with children and young people outside New Zealand
sections.fullText: 144A Sexual conduct with children and young people outside New Zealand 1 Every one commits an offence who, being a New Zealand citizen or ordinarily resident in New Zealand,— a does outside New Zealand, with or on a child under the age of 12 years, an act to which subsection (2) applies; or b does outside New Zealand, with or on a person under the age of 16 years, an act to which subsection (3) applies; or c does outside New Zealand, with or on a person under the age of 18 years, an act to which subsection (4) applies. 2 This subsection applies to an act that, if done in New Zealand, would be an offence against— a (sexual connection with a child under 12); or b (attempted sexual connection with a child under 12); or c (doing an indecent act on a child under 12). 3 This subsection applies to an act that, if done in New Zealand, would be an offence against— a (sexual connection with a young person); or b (attempted sexual connection with a young person); or c (doing an indecent act on a young person). 4 This subsection applies to an act that, if done in New Zealand, would be an offence against(breach of prohibitions on use in prostitution of persons under 18 years). 5 A person who commits an offence against this section in respect of a provision specified in any of subsections (2) to (4) is liable to the penalty to which he or she would be liable if convicted of an offence against the provision. 6 Every limiting provision that applied to a provision specified in any of subsections (2) to (4) when an offence against this section in respect of the provision specified is alleged to have been committed applies also to— a the commencement of proceedings for the offence; and b a charge under this section in respect of the provision specified. 7 In this section,, in relation to a provision specified in any of subsections (2) to (4), means a provision of this Act or thethat states (in relation to the provision specified only, or more generally)— a circumstances that constitute a defence to a charge under the provision specified; or b circumstances that do not constitute a defence to a charge under the provision specified; or c circumstances in which the person on or with whom an offence against the provision specified is committed may not be charged with an offence against that provision. :, on, byof the(2005 No 41).

sections.heading: Party or accessory liability for sexual acts with children or young people outside New Zealand done by, or involving, foreigner principal parties
sections.fullText: 144AB Party or accessory liability for sexual acts with children or young people outside New Zealand done by, or involving, foreigner principal parties 1 A New Zealander is a party under, an accessory after the fact, or both, to a New Zealand sexual offence with a child or young person outside New Zealand if,— a a sexual act with a child or young person is done or to be done, outside New Zealand, by 1 or more principal parties who are foreigners; and b the New Zealander does, outside New Zealand, an act with, or in respect of, the act done or to be done by the 1 or more foreigners; and c had both acts been done in New Zealand, the New Zealander would be a party under, an accessory after the fact, or both, to a sexual offence with a child or young person. 2 In this section,— means an accessory after the fact under means a person who— a is not a New Zealand citizen; and b is not a person ordinarily resident in New Zealand means an offence against means a person who— a is a New Zealand citizen; or b is a person ordinarily resident in New Zealand includes a party under that section as extended by means an act to whichapplies means an offence against a section specified in. 3 This section does not limit or affect(whether used with, or apart from,). Criminal Code Act 1995 Division 272 (Aust); Sexual Offences Act 2003 s 72, Schedule 2 (UK) :, on, byof the(2015 No 44).

sections.heading: Consent of Attorney-General required
sections.fullText: 144B Consent of Attorney-General required 1 Nofor an offence againstexcept with the consent of the Attorney-General. 2 A person who is alleged to have committed such an offence may be arrested, or a warrant for the arrest of the person may be issued and executed, and any such person may be remanded in custody or on bail, notwithstanding that the consent of the Attorney-General to thefor the offence has not been obtained, but no further or other proceedings shall be taken until that consent has been obtained. 3 The Attorney-General may, before deciding whether or not to give his or her consent under subsection (1), make such inquiries as he or she thinks fit. :, on, by section 2 of the(1995 No 49). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85).

sections.heading: Organising or promoting child sex tours
sections.fullText: 144C Organising or promoting child sex tours 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who— a makes or organises any travel arrangements for or on behalf of any other person with the intention of facilitating the commission by that other person of an offence against, whether or not such an offence is actually committed by that other person; or b transports any other person to a place outside New Zealand with the intention of facilitating the commission by that other person of an offence against, whether or not such an offence is actually committed by that other person; or c prints or publishes any information that is intended to promote conduct that would constitute an offence against, or to assist any other person to engage in such conduct. 2 For the purposes of this section,— a the making or organising of travel arrangements includes, but is not limited to,— i the purchase or reservation of tickets for travel to a country outside New Zealand: ii the purchase or reservation of accommodation in a country outside New Zealand: b the publication of information means publication of information by any means, whether by written, electronic, or other form of communication; and includes the distribution of information. :, on, by section 2 of the(1995 No 49).

sections.heading: Criminal nuisance
sections.fullText: 145 Criminal nuisance 1 Every one commits criminal nuisance who does any unlawful act or omits to discharge any legal duty, such act or omission being one which he or she knew would endanger the lives, safety, or health of the public, or the life, safety, or health of any individual. 2 Every one who commits criminal nuisance is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year. 1908 No 32 ss 158, 159
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"145","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Criminal nuisance","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one commits criminal nuisance who does any unlawful act or omits to discharge any legal duty, such act or omission being one which he or she knew would endanger the lives, safety, or health of the public, or the life, safety, or health of any individual."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who commits criminal nuisance is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 ss 158, 159","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329273","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Misconduct in respect of human remains
sections.fullText: 150 Misconduct in respect of human remains Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who— a neglects to perform any duty imposed on him or her by law or undertaken by him or her with reference to the burial or cremation of any dead human body or human remains; or b improperly or indecently interferes with or offers any indignity to any dead human body or human remains, whether buried or not. 1908 No 32 s 165
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"150","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Misconduct in respect of human remains","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"neglects to perform any duty imposed on him or her by law or undertaken by him or her with reference to the burial or cremation of any dead human body or human remains; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"improperly or indecently interferes with or offers any indignity to any dead human body or human remains, whether buried or not."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 165","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329287","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Standard of care applicable to persons under legal duties or performing unlawful acts
sections.fullText: 150A Standard of care applicable to persons under legal duties or performing unlawful acts 1 This section applies in respect of— a the legal duties specified in any of; and b an unlawful act referred to inwhere the unlawful act relied on requires proof of negligence or is a strict or absolute liability offence. 2 For the purposes of this Part, a person is criminally responsible for omitting to discharge or perform a legal duty, or performing an unlawful act, to which this section applies only if, in the circumstances, the omission or unlawful act is a major departure from the standard of care expected of a reasonable person to whom that legal duty applies or who performs that unlawful act. :, on, byof the(2011 No 79).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"150A","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Standard of care applicable to persons under legal duties or performing unlawful acts","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"This section applies in respect of—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":[{"#text":"sections 151","@_href":"DLM329292","@_id":"DLM4360885"},{"#text":"152","@_href":"DLM329293","@_id":"DLM4360886"},{"#text":"153","@_href":"DLM329294","@_id":"LMS627630"},{"#text":"155","@_href":"DLM329296","@_id":"LMS627631"},{"#text":"156","@_href":"DLM329297","@_id":"LMS627632"},{"#text":"157","@_href":"DLM329298","@_id":"LMS627633"}],"#text":",,,,, and","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"the legal duties specified in any of; and"}}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 160","@_href":"DLM329302","@_id":"DLM4360891"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"an unlawful act referred to inwhere the unlawful act relied on requires proof of negligence or is a strict or absolute liability offence."}}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"For the purposes of this Part, a person is criminally responsible for omitting to discharge or perform a legal duty, or performing an unlawful act, to which this section applies only if, in the circumstances, the omission or unlawful act is a major departure from the standard of care expected of a reasonable person to whom that legal duty applies or who performs that unlawful act."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 150A","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"19 March 2012","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 6","@_href":"DLM3650020","@_id":"DLM4360869"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 3) 2011","#text":":, on, byof the(2011 No 79).","@_id":"DLM4360852"}}},"@_id":"DLM329290","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty to provide necessaries and protect from injury
sections.fullText: 151 Duty to provide necessaries and protect from injury Every one who has actual care or charge of a person who is a vulnerable adult and who is unable to provide himself or herself with necessaries is under a legal duty— a to provide that person with necessaries; and b to take reasonable steps to protect that person from injury. :, on, byof the(2011 No 79).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"151","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty to provide necessaries and protect from injury","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who has actual care or charge of a person who is a vulnerable adult and who is unable to provide himself or herself with necessaries is under a legal duty—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to provide that person with necessaries; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to take reasonable steps to protect that person from injury."}]}]}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 151","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"19 March 2012","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 6","@_href":"DLM3650020","@_id":"DLM4360870"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 3) 2011","#text":":, on, byof the(2011 No 79).","@_id":"DLM4360853"}}},"@_id":"DLM329292","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty of parent or guardian to provide necessaries and protect from injury
sections.fullText: 152 Duty of parent or guardian to provide necessaries and protect from injury Every one who is a parent, or is a person in place of a parent, who has actual care or charge of a child under the age of 18 years is under a legal duty— a to provide that child with necessaries; and b to take reasonable steps to protect that child from injury. :, on, byof the(2011 No 79).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"152","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty of parent or guardian to provide necessaries and protect from injury","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who is a parent, or is a person in place of a parent, who has actual care or charge of a child under the age of 18 years is under a legal duty—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to provide that child with necessaries; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to take reasonable steps to protect that child from injury."}]}]}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 152","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"19 March 2012","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 6","@_href":"DLM3650020","@_id":"DLM4360871"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act (No 3) 2011","#text":":, on, byof the(2011 No 79).","@_id":"DLM4360854"}}},"@_id":"DLM329293","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty of employers to provide necessaries
sections.fullText: 153 Duty of employers to provide necessaries 1 Every one who as employer has contracted to provide necessary food, clothing, or lodging for any servant or apprentice under the age of 16 years is under a legal duty to provide the same, and is criminally responsible for omitting without lawful excuse to perform such duty if the death of that servant or apprentice is caused, or if his or her life is endangered or his or her health permanently injured, by such omission. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who, without lawful excuse, neglects the duty specified in this section so that the life of the servant or apprentice is endangered or his or her health permanently injured by such neglect. 1908 No 32 s 168
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"153","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty of employers to provide necessaries","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who as employer has contracted to provide necessary food, clothing, or lodging for any servant or apprentice under the age of 16 years is under a legal duty to provide the same, and is criminally responsible for omitting without lawful excuse to perform such duty if the death of that servant or apprentice is caused, or if his or her life is endangered or his or her health permanently injured, by such omission."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who, without lawful excuse, neglects the duty specified in this section so that the life of the servant or apprentice is endangered or his or her health permanently injured by such neglect."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 168","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329294","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Abandoning child under 6
sections.fullText: 154 Abandoning child under 6 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who unlawfully abandons or exposes any child under the age of 6 years. 1908 No 32 s 169
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"154","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Abandoning child under 6","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who unlawfully abandons or exposes any child under the age of 6 years."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 169","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329295","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty of persons doing dangerous acts
sections.fullText: 155 Duty of persons doing dangerous acts Every one who undertakes (except in case of necessity) to administer surgical or medical treatment, or to do any other lawful act the doing of which is or may be dangerous to life, is under a legal duty to have and to use reasonable knowledge, skill, and care in doing any such act, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty. 1908 No 32 s 170
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"155","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty of persons doing dangerous acts","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who undertakes (except in case of necessity) to administer surgical or medical treatment, or to do any other lawful act the doing of which is or may be dangerous to life, is under a legal duty to have and to use reasonable knowledge, skill, and care in doing any such act, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 170","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329296","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty of persons in charge of dangerous things
sections.fullText: 156 Duty of persons in charge of dangerous things Every one who has in his or her charge or under his or her control anything whatever, whether animate or inanimate, or who erects, makes, operates, or maintains anything whatever, which, in the absence of precaution or care, may endanger human life is under a legal duty to take reasonable precautions against and to use reasonable care to avoid such danger, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty. 1908 No 32 s 171
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"156","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty of persons in charge of dangerous things","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who has in his or her charge or under his or her control anything whatever, whether animate or inanimate, or who erects, makes, operates, or maintains anything whatever, which, in the absence of precaution or care, may endanger human life is under a legal duty to take reasonable precautions against and to use reasonable care to avoid such danger, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 171","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329297","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Duty to avoid omissions dangerous to life
sections.fullText: 157 Duty to avoid omissions dangerous to life Every one who undertakes to do any act the omission to do which is or may be dangerous to life is under a legal duty to do that act, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty. 1908 No 32 s 172
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"157","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Duty to avoid omissions dangerous to life","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who undertakes to do any act the omission to do which is or may be dangerous to life is under a legal duty to do that act, and is criminally responsible for the consequences of omitting without lawful excuse to discharge that duty."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 172","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329298","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Homicide defined
sections.fullText: 158 Homicide defined Homicide is the killing of a human being by another, directly or indirectly, by any means whatsoever. 1908 No 32 s 173
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"158","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Homicide defined","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Homicide is the killing of a human being by another, directly or indirectly, by any means whatsoever."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 173","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329300","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Killing of a child
sections.fullText: 159 Killing of a child 1 A child becomes a human being within the meaning of this Act when it has completely proceeded in a living state from the body of its mother, whether it has breathed or not, whether it has an independent circulation or not, and whether the navel string is severed or not. 2 The killing of such child is homicide if it dies in consequence of injuries received before, during, or after birth. 1908 No 32 s 174
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"159","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Killing of a child","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"A child becomes a human being within the meaning of this Act when it has completely proceeded in a living state from the body of its mother, whether it has breathed or not, whether it has an independent circulation or not, and whether the navel string is severed or not."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"The killing of such child is homicide if it dies in consequence of injuries received before, during, or after birth."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 174","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329301","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Culpable homicide
sections.fullText: 160 Culpable homicide 1 Homicide may be either culpable or not culpable. 2 Homicide is culpable when it consists in the killing of any person— a by an unlawful act; or b by an omission without lawful excuse to perform or observe any legal duty; or c by both combined; or d by causing that person by threats or fear of violence, or by deception, to do an act which causes his or her death; or e by wilfully frightening a child under the age of 16 years or a sick person. 3 Except as provided in, culpable homicide is either murder or manslaughter. 4 Homicide that is not culpable is not an offence. 1908 No 32 s 175
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"160","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Culpable homicide","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Homicide may be either culpable or not culpable."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Homicide is culpable when it consists in the killing of any person—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"by an unlawful act; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"by an omission without lawful excuse to perform or observe any legal duty; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"by both combined; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"d","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"by causing that person by threats or fear of violence, or by deception, to do an act which causes his or her death; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"e","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"by wilfully frightening a child under the age of 16 years or a sick person."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 178","@_href":"DLM329332","@_id":"DLM823471"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"Except as provided in, culpable homicide is either murder or manslaughter."}}]},{"label":{"#text":"4","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Homicide that is not culpable is not an offence."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 175","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329302","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Death must be within a year and a day

sections.heading: Killing by influence on the mind
sections.fullText: 163 Killing by influence on the mind No one is criminally responsible for the killing of another by any influence on the mind alone, except by wilfully frightening a child under the age of 16 years or a sick person, nor for the killing of another by any disorder or disease arising from such influence, except by wilfully frightening any such child as aforesaid or a sick person. 1908 No 32 s 178
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"163","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Killing by influence on the mind","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"No one is criminally responsible for the killing of another by any influence on the mind alone, except by wilfully frightening a child under the age of 16 years or a sick person, nor for the killing of another by any disorder or disease arising from such influence, except by wilfully frightening any such child as aforesaid or a sick person."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 178","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329306","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Acceleration of death
sections.fullText: 164 Acceleration of death Every one who by any act or omission causes the death of another person kills that person, although the effect of the bodily injury caused to that person was merely to hasten his or her death while labouring under some disorder or disease arising from some other cause. 1908 No 32 s 179
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"164","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Acceleration of death","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who by any act or omission causes the death of another person kills that person, although the effect of the bodily injury caused to that person was merely to hasten his or her death while labouring under some disorder or disease arising from some other cause."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 179","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329307","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Causing death that might have been prevented
sections.fullText: 165 Causing death that might have been prevented Every one who by any act or omission causes the death of another person kills that person, although death from that cause might have been prevented by resorting to proper means. 1908 No 32 s 180
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"165","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Causing death that might have been prevented","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who by any act or omission causes the death of another person kills that person, although death from that cause might have been prevented by resorting to proper means."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 180","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329308","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Causing injury the treatment of which causes death
sections.fullText: 166 Causing injury the treatment of which causes death Every one who causes to another person any bodily injury, in itself of a dangerous nature, from which death results, kills that person, although the immediate cause of death be treatment, proper or improper, applied in good faith. 1908 No 32 s 181
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"166","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Causing injury the treatment of which causes death","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who causes to another person any bodily injury, in itself of a dangerous nature, from which death results, kills that person, although the immediate cause of death be treatment, proper or improper, applied in good faith."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1908 No 32 s 181","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"@_id":"DLM329309","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Murder defined
sections.fullText: 167 Murder defined Culpable homicide is murder in each of the following cases: a if the offender means to cause the death of the person killed: b if the offender means to cause to the person killed any bodily injury that is known to the offender to be likely to cause death, and is reckless whether death ensues or not: c if the offender means to cause death, or, being so reckless as aforesaid, means to cause such bodily injury as aforesaid to one person, and by accident or mistake kills another person, though he or she does not mean to hurt the person killed: d if the offender for any unlawful object does an act that he or she knows to be likely to cause death, and thereby kills any person, though he or she may have desired that his or her object should be effected without hurting any one. 1908 No 32 s 182

sections.heading: Further definition of murder
sections.fullText: 168 Further definition of murder 1 Culpable homicide is also murder in each of the following cases, whether the offender means or does not mean death to ensue, or knows or does not know that death is likely to ensue: a if he or she means to cause grievous bodily injury for the purpose of facilitating the commission of any of the offences mentioned in subsection (2), or facilitating the flight or avoiding the detection of the offender upon the commission or attempted commission thereof, or for the purpose of resisting lawful apprehension in respect of any offence whatsoever, and death ensues from such injury: b if he or she administers any stupefying or overpowering thing for any of the purposes aforesaid, and death ensues from the effects thereof: c if he or she by any means wilfully stops the breath of any person for any of the purposes aforesaid, and death ensues from such stopping of breath. 2 The offences referred to in subsection (1) are those specified in the following provisions of this Act, namely: a (Treason) or(): aa (Improper conduct for or on behalf of foreign power): b (Sabotage): c (Piracy): d (Piratical acts): e (escape or rescue fromor lawful custody or detention): f (Sexual violation): g (Murder): h (abduction): i (Kidnapping): j (Burglary): k (Robbery): l (Arson). 1908 No 32 s 183 :, on, byof the(2025 No 71). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Manslaughter
sections.fullText: 171 Manslaughter Except as provided in, culpable homicide not amounting to murder is manslaughter. 1908 No 32 s 186

sections.heading: Punishment of murder
sections.fullText: 172 Punishment of murder 1 Every one who commits murder is liable to imprisonment for life. 2 Subsection (1) is subject to. :, on, byof the(2002 No 9).
