sections.heading: Prohibition on use of
sections.fullText: 216B Prohibition on use of 1 Subject to subsections (2), every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who intentionally intercepts any private communication by means of. 2 Subsection (1) does not apply where the person intercepting the private communication— a is a party to that private communication; or b does so pursuant to, and in accordance with the terms of, any authority conferred on him or her by or under— i the; or ii ; or iii the. 3 4 Subsection (1) does not apply to. 5 Subsection (1) does not apply to the interception of private communications by any interception device operated by a person engaged in providing an Internet or other communication service to the public if— a the interception is carried out by an employee of the person providing that Internet or other communication service to the public in the course of that person's duties; and b the interception is carried out for the purpose of maintaining that Internet or other communication service; and c the interception is necessary for the purpose of maintaining the Internet or other communication service; and d the interception is only used for the purpose of maintaining the Internet or other communication service. 6 Information obtained under subsection (5) must be destroyed immediately if it is no longer needed for the purpose of maintaining the Internet or other communication service. 7 :, on, by section 2 of the(1979 No 5). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, by section 10(1) of the(1999 No 114). :, on, byof the(1997 No 93). :, on, byof the(2017 No 10). :, on, byof the(2012 No 24). :, on, by section 10(2) of the(1999 No 114). :, on, byof the(2024 No 41). :, on, byof the(2009 No 3). :, on, byof the(2009 No 3). :, on, byof the(2004 No 50). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39).

sections.heading: Prohibition on disclosure of private communications unlawfully intercepted
sections.fullText: 216C Prohibition on disclosure of private communications unlawfully intercepted 1 Subject to subsection (2), where a private communication has been intercepted in contravention of, every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who intentionally— if he or she knows that it has come to his or her knowledge as a direct or indirect result of a contravention of. a discloses the private communication, or the substance, meaning, or purport of the communication, or any part of it; or b discloses the existence of the private communication,— 2 Subsection (1) does not apply where the disclosure is made— a to a party to the communication, or with the express or implied consent of such a party; or b in the course, or for the purpose, of— i an investigation by the Police into an alleged offence against this section or; or ii giving evidence in any civil or criminal proceedings relating to the unlawful interception of a private communication by means ofor the unlawful disclosure of a private communication unlawfully intercepted by that means; or iii giving evidence in any other civil or criminal proceeding where that evidence is not rendered inadmissible by theoror any other enactment or rule of law; or iv determining whether the disclosure is admissible in any civil or criminal proceedings. :, on, by section 2 of the(1979 No 5). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2006 No 69).

sections.heading: Prohibition on dealing, etc, with
sections.fullText: 216D Prohibition on dealing, etc, with 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who— — a invites any other person to acquire from him or her; or b offers or exposes for sale or supply to any other person; or c agrees to sell or supply or sells or supplies to any other person; or d has in his or her possession for the purpose of sale or supply to any other person,— i the sole or principal purpose of which he or she knows to be the surreptitious interception of private communications; or ii that he or she holds out as being useful for the surreptitious interception of private communications (whether or not he or she also holds it out as being useful for any other purpose). 2 It is a defence to a charge under this section if the person charged proves either— a that at the time he or she did any act referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (d) of subsection (1) he or she believed that the other person referred to in the relevant paragraph was a, or anof the New Zealand Security Intelligence Service, acting in the course of his or her official duties; or b where the charge relates to the supply ofotherwise than for valuable consideration, that— i he or she suppliedto the other person referred to in paragraph (c) or paragraph (d) of subsection (1) for the purpose of any proceeding or of any investigation or examination preliminary or incidental to any proceeding; or ii being aor anof the New Zealand Security Intelligence Service, he or she suppliedin the course of his or her official duties to the other person referred to in the said paragraph (c) or the said paragraph (d) for any lawful purpose. :, on, by section 2 of the(1979 No 5). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2017 No 10). :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72). :, on, byof the(2003 No 9). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2017 No 10). :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2003 No 9).

sections.heading: Forfeiture
sections.fullText: 216E Forfeiture Where any person is convicted of a crime againstin respect of anythe sole or principal purpose of which is the surreptitious interception of private communications, the court may, as part of the sentence, order that theshall be forfeited; and, in such a case, theshall thereupon become forfeited to the Crown accordingly, and may be disposed of in such manner as the Commissioner of Police directs. :, on, by section 2 of the(1979 No 5). :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Unlawful disclosure
sections.fullText: 216F Unlawful disclosure 1 An unlawful disclosure is the intentional and unauthorised disclosure of any information gained when undertaking maintenance of a communication service. 2 Despite anything in, a person may disclose information to anyif the information appears to relate to the commission ofthat has caused or could cause serious harm to any person. 3 Every person who makes an unlawful disclosure is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years. :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2012 No 24). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27). :, on, byof the(2012 No 24). :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72).

sections.heading: Intimate visual recording defined
sections.fullText: 216G Intimate visual recording defined 1 In,means a visual recording (for example, a photograph, videotape, or digital image) that is made in any medium using any device without the knowledge or consent of the person who is the subject of the recording, and the recording is of— a a person who is in a place which, in the circumstances, would reasonably be expected to provide privacy, and that person is— i naked or has his or her genitals, pubic area, buttocks, or female breasts exposed, partially exposed, or clad solely in undergarments; or ii engaged in an intimate sexual activity; or iii engaged in showering, toileting, or other personal bodily activity that involves dressing or undressing; or b a person's naked or undergarment-clad genitals, pubic area, buttocks, or female breasts which is made— i from beneath or under a person's clothing; or ii through a person's outer clothing in circumstances where it is unreasonable to do so. 2 In, intimate visual recording includes an intimate visual recording that is made and transmitted in real time without retention or storage in— a a physical form; or b an electronic form from which the recording is capable of being reproduced with or without the aid of any device or thing. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75).

sections.heading: Prohibition on making intimate visual recording
sections.fullText: 216H Prohibition on making intimate visual recording Everyone is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who intentionally or recklessly makes an intimate visual recording of another person. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"216H","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Prohibition on making intimate visual recording","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Everyone is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who intentionally or recklessly makes an intimate visual recording of another person."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 216H","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"5 December 2006","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 4","@_href":"DLM398244","@_id":"DLM1917629"},"amending-leg":"Crimes (Intimate Covert Filming) Amendment Act 2006","#text":":, on, byof the(2006 No 75).","@_id":"DLM1887950"}}},"@_id":"DLM329855","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Prohibition on possessing intimate visual recording in certain circumstances
sections.fullText: 216I Prohibition on possessing intimate visual recording in certain circumstances 1 Everyone is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who has in his or her possession an intimate visual recording— a for the purpose of publishing, exporting or selling the intimate visual recording; and b knowing it to be an intimate visual recording, or being reckless as to whether it is an intimate visual recording. 2 Everyone is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year who, without reasonable excuse, has in his or her possession an intimate visual recording knowing it to be an intimate visual recording. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75).

sections.heading: Prohibition on publishing, importing, exporting, or selling intimate visual recording
sections.fullText: 216J Prohibition on publishing, importing, exporting, or selling intimate visual recording 1 Everyone is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, knowing that a visual recording is an intimate visual recording, or being reckless as to whether a visual recording is an intimate visual recording,— a publishes in New Zealand the intimate visual recording: b imports into New Zealand the intimate visual recording: c exports from New Zealand the intimate visual recording: d sells the intimate visual recording. 2 In this section, unless the context otherwise requires,— means any of the following: a displays by any means: b sends to any person by any means: c distributes by any means: d conveys by electronic medium: e stores electronically in a way that is accessible by any other person or persons means sells in a physical form or by electronic medium, and includes— a offers for sale: b agrees to sell. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75).

sections.heading: Exceptions to prohibition in section 216J
sections.fullText: 216K Exceptions to prohibition in section 216J 1 Nothing inapplies to anything done by any person in the course of, or in connection with, exercising or performing any powers, duties, or functions under any enactment. 2 Nothing inapplies to any person by reason only of that person publishing an intimate visual recording to a person referred to in. 3 Nothing inapplies to any person who, not knowing or suspecting that a visual recording is an intimate visual recording, facilitates access to that recording by reason only of providing some or all of the means necessary for— a delivery of the recording in physical form (for example, by a postal operator or courier); or b transmission (other than by broadcasting) of the recording (for example, by a network operator or service provider providing only a network or facility through which a recording is transmitted); or c storage of the recording electronically in a way that is accessible by any other person or persons. 4 In subsection (3), unless the context otherwise requires,— means a person carrying on business as a courier has the same meaning as in has the same meaning as in has the same meaning as in — a means a person providing Internet access, email access, or both of those facilities, by means of a public data network; but b does not include a network operator. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75). :, on, byof the(2018 No 48).

sections.heading: Disposal and forfeiture
sections.fullText: 216L Disposal and forfeiture 1 Where any person is convicted of an offence againstoror, the court may, in addition to or instead of passing any other sentence or making any other order in respect of the offence, order that the intimate visual recording be destroyed within 10 working days from the making of the order, and that the recording in the meantime be impounded. 2 Where any person is convicted of an offence againstoror, the court may, in addition to or instead of passing any other sentence or making any other order in respect of the offence, order that any equipment, goods, or other thing used in respect of the commission of the offence be forfeited to the Crown; and anything so forfeited must be sold, destroyed, or otherwise disposed of as the Minister of Justice directs. 3 Before making an order under subsection (1) or subsection (2), the court must give the following persons an opportunity to be heard: a the person convicted; and b any other person who, in the opinion of the court, would be directly affected by the making of the order. 4 If the court is satisfied that the intimate visual recording that was the subject of the prosecution, or any equipment, goods, or other thing used in respect of the commission of the offence, should be delivered to a person other than the person convicted, it may so order. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75).

sections.heading: Effect of appeal on order made under section 216L
sections.fullText: 216M Effect of appeal on order made under section 216L 1 If any person is convicted of an offence againstoror, and any order is made under, the operation of the order is suspended,— a in any case, until the expiration of the time prescribed in theor this Act for the filing of a notice of appeal or an application for leave to appeal; and b if a notice of appeal is filed within the time so prescribed, until the determination of the appeal; and c if the application for leave to appeal is filed within the time so prescribed, until the application is determined, and, if leave to appeal is granted, until the determination of the appeal. 2 If the operation of any order is suspended until the determination of the appeal, the court determining the appeal may, by order, cancel or vary the order. :, on, byof the(2006 No 75). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85).

sections.heading: Protection from liability
sections.fullText: 216N Protection from liability 1 This section applies to the following persons: a any; and b any Customs officer; and c anyemployee of the New Zealand Security Intelligence Service; and ca any employee of the Government Communications Security Bureau; and d any employee of the Department of Corrections and any other employee exercising powers or functions under the; and e any lawyer or agent— i giving legal advice in relation to an intimate visual recording; or ii giving legal advice, or making representations, in relation to any civil or criminal proceedings; and f any other person or member of a class of persons prescribed in regulations made under this Act; and g the person who is the subject of an intimate visual recording. 2 No person referred to in subsection (1) (g) commits an offence againstorby possessing or publishing the intimate visual recording. 3 No other person to whom this section applies commits an offence againstororby making, possessing, or publishing any intimate visual recording for the purpose of, or in the course of, carrying out the functions set out in subsection (5). 4 Subsection (3) does not apply in respect of anything done in bad faith or without reasonable cause. 5 The functions referred to in subsection (3) are functions relating to— a the prevention, detection, investigation, prosecution, or punishment of offences: b the conduct of proceedings in any court or tribunal: c security or safety. 6 Subsections (1) to (3) apply despite the fact that, but for this section, the act would have otherwise constituted an offence. 7 Where a person to whom subsection (1) (a) to (f) applies has in his or her possession an intimate visual recording, the recording must not be kept longer than is required for the purpose of carrying out the functions referred to in subsection (5), and must then either be— a made available to the person who is the subject of the recording, if that person requests; or b immediately destroyed if the person who is the subject of the recording makes no request under paragraph (a). :, on, byof the(2006 No 75). :, on, pursuant toof the(2008 No 72). :, on, byof the(2017 No 10). :, on, byof the(2017 No 10).

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.fullText: 217 Interpretation In this Part, unless the context otherwise requires,— , in relation to an act or omission, means done or omitted without a belief that there was express or implied consent to, or authority for, the act or omission from a person entitled to give such consent or authority means a document, or part of a document, in any form; and includes, without limitation,— a any paper or other material used for writing or printing that is marked with matter capable of being read; or b any photograph, or any photographic negative, plate, slide, film, or microfilm, or any photostatic negative; or c any disc, tape, wire, sound track, card, or other material or device in or on which information, sounds, or other data are recorded, stored (whether temporarily or permanently), or embodied so as to be capable, with or without the aid of some other equipment, of being reproduced; or d any material by means of which information is supplied, whether directly or by means of any equipment, to any device used for recording or storing or processing information; or e any material derived, whether directly or by means of any equipment, from information recorded or stored or processed by any device used for recording or storing or processing information , in relation to any person, means obtain or retain for himself or herself or for any other person. 1961 No 43 ss 217,, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Matters of ownership
sections.fullText: 218 Matters of ownership 1 For the purposes of this Part, a person is to be regarded as the owner of any property that is stolen if, at the time of the theft, that person has— a possession or control of the property; or b any interest in the property; or c the right to take possession or control of the property. 2 An owner of any property may be guilty of theft against another owner of that property. 3 All living creatures wild by nature, such as are not commonly found in a condition of natural liberty in New Zealand, are, if kept in a state of confinement, capable of being stolen, not only while so confined, but after they have escaped from confinement. 4 All other living creatures wild by nature are, if kept in a state of confinement, capable of being stolen so long as they remain in confinement, or are being pursued upon escaping from confinement. 5 A wild living creature is in a state of confinement so long as it is in an enclosure designed to prevent escape, or otherwise secured, and to allow its owner to take possession of it when he or she pleases. 6 Shellfish of all types are capable of being stolen when in oyster beds, marine farms, layings, and fisheries that are the property of any person and that are sufficiently marked out or shown as such property. 1961 No 43, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

remain in confinement, or are being pursued upon escaping from confinement."}]},{"label":{"#text":"5","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"A wild living creature is in a state of confinement so long as it is in an enclosure designed to prevent escape, or otherwise secured, and to allow its owner to take possession of it when he or she pleases."}]},{"label":{"#text":"6","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Shellfish of all types are capable of being stolen when in oyster beds, marine farms, layings, and fisheries that are the property of any person and that are sufficiently marked out or shown as such property."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":[{"#text":"ss 219","@_href":"DLM329897","@_id":"DLM824964"},{"#text":"225","@_href":"DLM330213","@_id":"DLM824965"}],"#text":"1961 No 43,","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 218","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917637"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887969"}}},"@_id":"DLM329895","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Theft or stealing
sections.fullText: 219 Theft or stealing 1 Theft or stealing is the act of,— a dishonestly and without claim of right, taking any property with intent to deprive any owner permanently of that property or of any interest in that property; or b dishonestly and without claim of right, using or dealing with any property with intent to deprive any owner permanently of that property or of any interest in that property after obtaining possession of, or control over, the property in whatever manner. 2 An intent to deprive any owner permanently of property includes an intent to deal with property in such a manner that— a the property cannot be returned to any owner in the same condition; or b any owner is likely to be permanently deprived of the property or of any interest in the property. 3 In this section,does not include obtaining ownership or possession of, or control over, any property with the consent of the person from whom it is obtained, whether or not consent is obtained by deception. 4 For tangible property, theft is committed by a taking when the offender moves the property or causes it to be moved. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"219","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Theft or stealing","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Theft or stealing is the act of,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"dishonestly and without claim of right, taking any property with intent to deprive any owner permanently of that property or of any interest in that property; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"dishonestly and without claim of right, using or dealing with any property with intent to deprive any owner permanently of that property or of any interest in that property after obtaining possession of, or control over, the property in whatever manner."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"An intent to deprive any owner permanently of property includes an intent to deal with property in such a manner that—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the property cannot be returned to any owner in the same condition; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any owner is likely to be permanently deprived of the property or of any interest in the property."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"def-term":{"#text":"taking","@_id":"DLM2073213"},"#text":"In this section,does not include obtaining ownership or possession of, or control over, any property with the consent of the person from whom it is obtained, whether or not consent is obtained by deception."}}]},{"label":{"#text":"4","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"For tangible property, theft is committed by a taking when the offender moves the property or causes it to be moved."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 220(1), (2), (5)","@_href":"DLM330201","@_id":"DLM824967"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 219","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917638"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887971"}}},"@_id":"DLM329897","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Theft by person in special relationship
sections.fullText: 220 Theft by person in special relationship 1 This section applies to any person who has received or is in possession of, or has control over, any property on terms or in circumstances that the person knows require the person— a to account to any other person for the property, or for any proceeds arising from the property; or b to deal with the property, or any proceeds arising from the property, in accordance with the requirements of any other person. 2 Every one to whom subsection (1) applies commits theft who intentionally fails to account to the other person as so required or intentionally deals with the property, or any proceeds of the property, otherwise than in accordance with those requirements. 3 This section applies whether or not the person was required to deliver over the identical property received or in the person's possession or control. 4 For the purposes of subsection (1), it is a question of law whether the circumstances required any person to account or to act in accordance with any requirements. 1961 No 43,, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Theft by employer
sections.fullText: 220AA Theft by employer 1 This section applies to a person () who— a employs another person (); and b is required to pay any money owed to B in relation to the employment under— i an employment agreement (whether or not the agreement is in writing); or ii an Act (for example, the, the, or the). 2 A commits theft if A intentionally fails, without reasonable excuse, to pay the money to B. 3 In this section,— , in relation to a homeworker, includes engage, andhas a corresponding meaning has the same meaning as in has the same meaning as in. :, on, byof the(2025 No 8).

sections.heading: Theft of livestock and other animals
sections.fullText: 220A Theft of livestock and other animals A person commits theft and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years if they steal any livestock or any other animal that is the property of any other person. :, on, byof the(2019 No 4).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"220A","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Theft of livestock and other animals","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"A person commits theft and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years if they steal any livestock or any other animal that is the property of any other person."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 220A","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"12 March 2019","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"LMS155086","@_id":"LMS170482"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2019","#text":":, on, byof the(2019 No 4).","@_id":"LMS170467"}}},"@_id":"LMS170466","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Theft of parts of killed animals
sections.fullText: 221 Theft of parts of killed animals Every one commits theft if he or she kills any animal that is the property of any other person with intent to steal the carcass, skin, or plumage, or any other part, of the animal. 1961 No 43 s 221 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2019 No 4).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"221","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Theft of parts of killed animals","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one commits theft if he or she kills any animal that is the property of any other person with intent to steal the carcass, skin, or plumage, or any other part, of the animal."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1961 No 43 s 221","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 221","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917640"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887975"},{"amended-provision":"Section 221 heading","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"12 March 2019","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 10","@_href":"LMS155094","@_id":"LMS170483"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2019","#text":":, on, byof the(2019 No 4).","@_id":"LMS170477"}]}},"@_id":"DLM330203","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Theft by spouse
sections.fullText: 222 Theft by spouse A person may be convicted of theft of another person's property even though those persons were married toeach other at the time of the theft. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2005 No 3). :, on, byof the(2005 No 3).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"222","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":{"insertwords":"or partner","#text":"Theft by spouse"},"prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":", or in a civil union or a de facto relationship with,","#text":"A person may be convicted of theft of another person's property even though those persons were married toeach other at the time of the theft."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 226","@_href":"DLM330215","@_id":"DLM824979"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 222","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917641"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887977"},{"amended-provision":"Section 222 heading","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"26 April 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM333795","@_id":"DLM824981"},"amending-leg":"Relationships (Statutory References) Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 3).","@_id":"DLM330207"},{"amended-provision":"Section 222","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"26 April 2005","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM333795","@_id":"DLM824982"},"amending-leg":"Relationships (Statutory References) Act 2005","#text":":, on, byof the(2005 No 3).","@_id":"DLM330208"}]}},"@_id":"DLM330205","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Punishment of theft
sections.fullText: 223 Punishment of theft Every one who commitsis liable as follows: a in the case of any offence against, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years; or b if the value of the property stolen exceeds $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years; or c if the value of the property stolen exceeds $500 but does not exceed $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year; or d if the value of the property stolen does not exceed $500, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2019 No 4).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"223","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Punishment of theft","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 220A","@_href":"LMS170466","@_id":"LMS170508"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"theft (except under)"},"#text":"Every one who commitsis liable as follows:"},"label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 220","@_href":"DLM330201","@_id":"DLM824983"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"#text":"in the case of any offence against, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years; or"}}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"if the value of the property stolen exceeds $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"if the value of the property stolen exceeds $500 but does not exceed $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"d","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"if the value of the property stolen does not exceed $500, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 227","@_href":"DLM330218","@_id":"DLM824984"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 223","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917642"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887979"},{"amended-provision":"Section 223","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"12 March 2019","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 11","@_href":"LMS155096","@_id":"LMS170519"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2019","#text":":, on, byof the(2019 No 4).","@_id":"LMS170518"}]}},"@_id":"DLM330209","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Conversion of vehicle or other conveyance
sections.fullText: 226 Conversion of vehicle or other conveyance 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, dishonestly and without claim of right, but not so as to be guilty of theft, takes or uses for his or her own purposes or another person's purposes— a any vehicle, ship, or aircraft; or b any part of any vehicle, ship, or aircraft; or c any horse. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who attempts to commit the offence in subsection (1) or who, dishonestly and without claim of right, interferes with, or gets into or upon, any vehicle, ship, or aircraft. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"226","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Conversion of vehicle or other conveyance","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, dishonestly and without claim of right, but not so as to be guilty of theft, takes or uses for his or her own purposes or another person's purposes—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any vehicle, ship, or aircraft; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any part of any vehicle, ship, or aircraft; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"any horse."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 2 years who attempts to commit the offence in subsection (1) or who, dishonestly and without claim of right, interferes with, or gets into or upon, any vehicle, ship, or aircraft."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 228","@_href":"DLM330230","@_id":"DLM825203"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 226","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917645"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887985"}}},"@_id":"DLM330215","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Being in possession of instrument for conversion
sections.fullText: 227 Being in possession of instrument for conversion Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year who, without lawful authority or excuse, has in his or her possession any instrument capable of being used for taking or converting any vehicle, ship, or aircraft with intent to use it for such a purpose. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"227","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Being in possession of instrument for conversion","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year who, without lawful authority or excuse, has in his or her possession any instrument capable of being used for taking or converting any vehicle, ship, or aircraft with intent to use it for such a purpose."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 229","@_href":"DLM330233","@_id":"DLM825205"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 227","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917646"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887987"}}},"@_id":"DLM330218","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Dishonestly taking or using document
sections.fullText: 228 Dishonestly taking or using document 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, with intent to obtain any property, service, pecuniary advantage, or valuable consideration,— a dishonestly and without claim of right, takes or obtains any document; or b dishonestly and without claim of right, uses or attempts to use any document. 2 Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without reasonable excuse, sells, transfers, or otherwise makes available any document knowing that— a the document was, dishonestly and without claim of right, taken, obtained, or used; and b the document was dealt with in the manner specified in paragraph (a) with intent to obtain any property, service, pecuniary advantage, or valuable consideration. 1961 No 43 s 229A :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95).

or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"dishonestly and without claim of right, uses or attempts to use any document."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without reasonable excuse, sells, transfers, or otherwise makes available any document knowing that—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the document was, dishonestly and without claim of right, taken, obtained, or used; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"the document was dealt with in the manner specified in paragraph (a) with intent to obtain any property, service, pecuniary advantage, or valuable consideration."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1961 No 43 s 229A","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 228","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917649"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887993"},{"amended-provision":"Section 228(2)","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"7 November 2015","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 10","@_href":"DLM6602023","@_id":"DLM6650935"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2015","#text":":, on, byof the(2015 No 95).","@_id":"DLM6650992"}]}},"@_id":"DLM330230","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Criminal breach of trust
sections.fullText: 229 Criminal breach of trust 1 Every one is guilty of a criminal breach of trust who, as a trustee of any trust, dishonestly and contrary to the terms of that trust, converts anything to any use not authorised by the trust. 2 Every trustee who commits a criminal breach of trust is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"229","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Criminal breach of trust","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is guilty of a criminal breach of trust who, as a trustee of any trust, dishonestly and contrary to the terms of that trust, converts anything to any use not authorised by the trust."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every trustee who commits a criminal breach of trust is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 230","@_href":"DLM330238","@_id":"DLM825219"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 229","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917650"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1887995"}}},"@_id":"DLM330233","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Taking, obtaining, or copying trade secrets
sections.fullText: 230 Taking, obtaining, or copying trade secrets 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who, with intent to obtain any pecuniary advantage or to cause loss to any other person,— a dishonestly and without claim of right, takes, obtains, or copies any document or any model or other depiction of any thing or process containing or embodying any trade secret, knowing that it contains or embodies a trade secret; or b dishonestly and without claim of right, takes or obtains any copy of any document or any model or other depiction of any thing or process containing or embodying any trade secret, knowing that it contains or embodies a trade secret. 2 For the purposes of this section,means any information that— a is, or has the potential to be, used industrially or commercially; and b is not generally available in industrial or commercial use; and c has economic value or potential economic value to the possessor of the information; and d is the subject of all reasonable efforts to preserve its secrecy. :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Burglary
sections.fullText: 231 Burglary 1 Every one commits burglary and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who— a enters any building or ship, or part of a building or ship, without authority and with intent to commitin the building or ship; or b having entered any building or ship, remains in it without authority and with intent to commitin the building or ship. 2 In this section and in,means any building or structure of any description, whether permanent or temporary; and includes a tent, caravan, or houseboat; and also includes any enclosed yard or any closed cave or closed tunnel. 3 For the purposes of this section and,— a entrance into a building or ship is made as soon as any part of the body of the person making the entrance, or any part of any instrument used by that person, is within the building or ship; and b every one who gains entrance to a building or ship by any threat or artifice used for that purpose is to be treated as having entered without authority. 1961 No 43 ss 240, 241, 242 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85).

sections.heading: Entry onto agricultural land with intent to commit imprisonable offence
sections.fullText: 231A Entry onto agricultural land with intent to commit imprisonable offence 1 A person commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years if they enter onto any land used for agricultural purposes, without authority and with intent to commit an imprisonable offence relating to any building or structure, livestock, animal, or machinery on that land. 2 For the purpose of this section,— a entry onto land occurs as soon as any part of the body of the person entering onto the land, or any part of any instrument used by that person, is within the boundaries of the land; and b everyone who gains entry onto land by any threat or artifice used for that purpose is to be treated as having entered without authority. 3 apply to this section with any necessary modifications. 4 For the purpose of this section,— includes (without limitation) the farming of livestock or crops, horticulture (including growing fruit, berries, or vegetables), viticulture, bee-keeping, and forest cultivation does not include a building within the meaning given in. :, on, byof the(2019 No 4).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"231A","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Entry onto agricultural land with intent to commit imprisonable offence","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"A person commits an offence and is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years if they enter onto any land used for agricultural purposes, without authority and with intent to commit an imprisonable offence relating to any building or structure, livestock, animal, or machinery on that land."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"For the purpose of this section,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"entry onto land occurs as soon as any part of the body of the person entering onto the land, or any part of any instrument used by that person, is within the boundaries of the land; sections.heading: Aggravated burglary
sections.fullText: 232 Aggravated burglary 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who,— a while committing burglary, has a weapon with him or her or uses any thing as a weapon; or b having committed burglary, has a weapon with him or her, or uses any thing as a weapon, while still in the building or ship. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who is armed with a weapon with intent to commit burglary. 1961 No 43 ss 240A, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"232","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Aggravated burglary","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"while committing burglary, has a weapon with him or her or uses any thing as a weapon; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"having committed burglary, has a weapon with him or her, or uses any thing as a weapon, while still in the building or ship."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who is armed with a weapon with intent to commit burglary."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"243","@_href":"DLM330289","@_id":"DLM825230"},"#text":"1961 No 43 ss 240A,","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 232","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917654"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891404"}}},"@_id":"DLM330245","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Being disguised or in possession of instrument for burglary
sections.fullText: 233 Being disguised or in possession of instrument for burglary 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without lawful authority or excuse,— a has in his or her possession any instrument capable of being used for burglary with intent to use it for such a purpose; or b has his or her face covered or is otherwise disguised with intent to commit any. 2 If any person is convicted of being in possession of an instrument for burglary, the court may, if it thinks fit, order the instrument to be forfeited to the Crown or disposed of as the court directs at the expense of the convicted person, and may order the person to pay any reasonable cost incurred by the Commissioner of Police in retaining the instrument. ; :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"233","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Being disguised or in possession of instrument for burglary","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without lawful authority or excuse,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"has in his or her possession any instrument capable of being used for burglary with intent to use it for such a purpose; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"insertwords":"imprisonable offence","#text":"has his or her face covered or is otherwise disguised with intent to commit any."}}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"If any person is convicted of being in possession of an instrument for burglary, the court may, if it thinks fit, order the instrument to be forfeited to the Crown or disposed of as the court directs at the expense of the convicted person, and may order the person to pay any reasonable cost incurred by the Commissioner of Police in retaining the instrument."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":[{"intref":{"#text":"s 244","@_href":"DLM330403","@_id":"DLM825232"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"},{"extref":{"#text":"s 97(d)","@_href":"DLM35242","@_id":"DLM825234"},"#text":"1999 No 93","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}],"#text":";"},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 233","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917655"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891406"},{"amended-provision":"Section 233(1)﻿(b)","amending-operation":"amended","amendment-date":"1 July 2013","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 7","@_href":"DLM5189023","@_id":"DLM5348251"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2013","#text":":, on, byof the(2013 No 27).","@_id":"DLM5347964"}]}},"@_id":"DLM330247","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Robbery
sections.fullText: 234 Robbery 1 Robbery is theft accompanied by violence or threats of violence, to any person or property, used to extort the property stolen or to prevent or overcome resistance to its being stolen. 2 Every one who commits robbery is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years. 1961 No 43 s 234 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"234","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Robbery","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Robbery is theft accompanied by violence or threats of violence, to any person or property, used to extort the property stolen or to prevent or overcome resistance to its being stolen."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who commits robbery is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1961 No 43 s 234","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 234","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917656"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891408"}}},"@_id":"DLM330250","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Aggravated robbery
sections.fullText: 235 Aggravated robbery Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who— a robs any person and, at the time of, or immediately before or immediately after, the robbery, causes grievous bodily harm to any person; or b being together with any other person or persons, robs any person; or c being armed with any offensive weapon or instrument, or any thing appearing to be such a weapon or instrument, robs any other person. 1961 No 43 s 235 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"235","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Aggravated robbery","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"robs any person and, at the time of, or immediately before or immediately after, the robbery, causes grievous bodily harm to any person; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"being together with any other person or persons, robs any person; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"being armed with any offensive weapon or instrument, or any thing appearing to be such a weapon or instrument, robs any other person."}]}]}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1961 No 43 s 235","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 235","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917657"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891410"}}},"@_id":"DLM330254","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Assault with intent to rob
sections.fullText: 236 Assault with intent to rob 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, with intent to rob any person,— a causes grievous bodily harm to that person or any other person; or b being armed with any offensive weapon or instrument, or any thing appearing to be such a weapon or instrument, assaults that person or any other person; or c being together with any other person or persons, assaults that person or any other person. 2 Every one who assaults any person with intent to rob that person or any other person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years. 1961 No 43, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"236","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Assault with intent to rob","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, with intent to rob any person,—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"causes grievous bodily harm to that person or any other person; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"being armed with any offensive weapon or instrument, or any thing appearing to be such a weapon or instrument, assaults that person or any other person; or"}]},{"label":{"#text":"c","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"being together with any other person or persons, assaults that person or any other person."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who assaults any person with intent to rob that person or any other person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":[{"#text":"ss 235(1)","@_href":"DLM330254","@_id":"DLM825252"},{"#text":"237","@_href":"DLM330263","@_id":"DLM825253"}],"#text":"1961 No 43,","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 236","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917658"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891412"}}},"@_id":"DLM330259","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Blackmail
sections.fullText: 237 Blackmail 1 Every one commits blackmail who threatens, expressly or by implication, to make any accusation against any person (whether living or dead), to disclose something about any person (whether living or dead), or to cause serious damage to property or endanger the safety of any person with intent— a to cause the person to whom the threat is made to act in accordance with the will of the person making the threat; and b to obtain any benefit or to cause loss to any other person. 2 Every one who acts in the manner described in subsection (1) is guilty of blackmail, even though that person believes that he or she is entitled to the benefit or to cause the loss, unless the making of the threat is, in the circumstances, a reasonable and proper means for effecting his or her purpose. 3 In this section and in,means any benefit, pecuniary advantage, privilege, property, service, or valuable consideration. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"237","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Blackmail","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one commits blackmail who threatens, expressly or by implication, to make any accusation against any person (whether living or dead), to disclose something about any person (whether living or dead), or to cause serious damage to property or endanger the safety of any person with intent—","label-para":[{"label":{"#text":"a","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to cause the person to whom the threat is made to act in accordance with the will of the person making the threat; and"}]},{"label":{"#text":"b","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"to obtain any benefit or to cause loss to any other person."}]}]}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who acts in the manner described in subsection (1) is guilty of blackmail, even though that person believes that he or she is entitled to the benefit or to cause the loss, unless the making of the threat is, in the circumstances, a reasonable and proper means for effecting his or her purpose."}]},{"label":{"#text":"3","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"section 239","@_href":"DLM330270","@_id":"DLM825262"},"@_jurisdiction":"nz"},"def-term":{"#text":"benefit","@_id":"DLM330264"},"#text":"In this section and in,means any benefit, pecuniary advantage, privilege, property, service, or valuable consideration."}}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"s 238","@_href":"DLM330268","@_id":"DLM825263"},"#text":"1961 No 43","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 237","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917659"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891414"}}},"@_id":"DLM330263","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Punishment of blackmail
sections.fullText: 238 Punishment of blackmail Every one who commits blackmail is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years. 1961 No 43 s 238(1) :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"238","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Punishment of blackmail","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"no"},"para":[{"text":"Every one who commits blackmail is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"#text":"1961 No 43 s 238(1)","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 238","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917660"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891416"}}},"@_id":"DLM330268","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Demanding with intent to steal, etc
sections.fullText: 239 Demanding with intent to steal, etc 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, without claim of right, by force or with any threat, compels any person to execute, make, accept, endorse, alter, or destroy any document capable of conferring a pecuniary advantage with intent to obtain any benefit. 2 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, with menaces or by any threat, demands any property from any persons with intent to steal it. 1961 No 43, 239 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"239","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Demanding with intent to steal, etc","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"#text":"1","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 14 years who, without claim of right, by force or with any threat, compels any person to execute, make, accept, endorse, alter, or destroy any document capable of conferring a pecuniary advantage with intent to obtain any benefit."}]},{"label":{"#text":"2","@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, with menaces or by any threat, demands any property from any persons with intent to steal it."}]}]},"cf":{"citation":{"intref":{"#text":"ss 236","@_href":"DLM330259","@_id":"DLM825273"},"#text":"1961 No 43, 239","@_jurisdiction":"nz"}},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":{"amended-provision":"Section 239","amending-operation":"replaced","amendment-date":"1 October 2003","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 15","@_href":"DLM200200","@_id":"DLM1917661"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2003","#text":":, on, byof the(2003 No 39).","@_id":"DLM1891418"}}},"@_id":"DLM330270","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Designing, manufacturing, or adapting goods with intent to facilitate commission of crimes involving dishonesty
sections.fullText: 239A Designing, manufacturing, or adapting goods with intent to facilitate commission of crimes involving dishonesty Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who designs, manufactures, or adapts goods with intent to facilitate the commission of a crime involving dishonesty. :, as section 228A, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39).
sections.rawXml: {"label":{"#text":"239A","@_denominator":"yes"},"heading":"Designing, manufacturing, or adapting goods with intent to facilitate commission of crimes involving dishonesty","prov.body":{"subprov":[{"label":{"@_denominator":"yes"},"para":[{"text":"Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who designs, manufactures, or adapts goods with intent to facilitate the commission of a crime involving dishonesty."}]}]},"notes":{"history":{"history-note":[{"amended-provision":"Section 239A","amending-operation":"inserted","amendment-date":"7 November 2015","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 11","@_href":"DLM6602024","@_id":"DLM6650939"},"amending-leg":"Crimes Amendment Act 2015","#text":":, as section 228A, on, byof the(2015 No 95).","@_id":"DLM6650993"},{"amended-provision":"Section 239A","amending-operation":"renumbered","amendment-date":"31 July 2025","amending-provision":{"#text":"section 60","@_href":"LMS991402","@_id":"LMS1464184"},"amending-leg":"Budapest Convention and Related Matters Legislation Amendment Act 2025","#text":":, on, byof the(2025 No 39).","@_id":"LMS1463215"}]}},"@_id":"DLM6650936","@_toc":"yes"}

sections.heading: Possessing, selling,goods designed, manufactured, or adapted with intent to facilitate commission of crimes involving dishonesty
sections.fullText: 239B Possessing, selling,goods designed, manufactured, or adapted with intent to facilitate commission of crimes involving dishonesty Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without lawful authority or excuse, possesses, sells,any goods designed, manufactured, or adapted to facilitate the commission of a crime involving dishonesty, with intent to use, or to enable another person to use, the goods to facilitate the commission of a crime involving dishonesty. :, as section 228B, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39).

sections.heading: Possessing goods capable of being used to facilitate crimes involving dishonesty with intent to facilitate commission of those offences
sections.fullText: 239C Possessing goods capable of being used to facilitate crimes involving dishonesty with intent to facilitate commission of those offences Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without lawful authority or excuse, possesses any goods capable of being used to facilitate the commission of a crime involving dishonesty (other than those specified in) with intent to use the goods to facilitate the commission of that offence. :, as section 228C, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39).

sections.heading: Obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception
sections.fullText: 240 Obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception 1 Every one is guilty of obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception who, by any deception and without claim of right,— a obtains ownership or possession of, or control over, any property, or any privilege, service, pecuniary advantage, benefit, or valuable consideration, directly or indirectly; or b in incurring any debt or liability, obtains credit; or c induces or causes any other person to deliver over, execute, make, accept, endorse, destroy, or alter any document or thing capable of being used to derive a pecuniary advantage; or d causes loss to any other person. 1A Every person is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 years who, without reasonable excuse, sells, transfers, or otherwise makes available any document or thing capable of being used to derive a pecuniary advantage knowing that, by deception and without claim of right, the document or thing was, or was caused to be, delivered, executed, made, accepted, endorsed, or altered. 2 In this section,means— a a false representation, whether oral, documentary, or by conduct, where the person making the representation intends to deceive any other person and— i knows that it is false in a material particular; or ii is reckless as to whether it is false in a material particular; or b an omission to disclose a material particular, with intent to deceive any person, in circumstances where there is a duty to disclose it; or c a fraudulent device, trick, or stratagem used with intent to deceive any person. 1961 No 43,, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95).

sections.heading: Application of section 240 to match-fixing
sections.fullText: 240A Application of section 240 to match-fixing 1 For the purposes of,includes any act or omission that is done or omitted with intent to influence a betting outcome of an activity of a kind to which subsection (2) applies by manipulating— a the overall result of the activity; or b any event within the activity. 2 This subsection applies to activities of the following kinds: a sporting competitions, games, matches, races, and rallies involving human participants (whether or not they also involve equipment, horses, vehicles, or vessels): b dog races. 3 This section does not limit or affect the generality of. :, on, byof the(2014 No 67).

sections.heading: Punishment of obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception
sections.fullText: 241 Punishment of obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception Every one who is guilty of obtaining by deception or causing loss by deception is liable as follows: a if the loss caused or the value of what is obtained or sought to be obtained exceeds $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years: b if the loss caused or the value of what is obtained or sought to be obtained exceeds $500 but does not exceed $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year: c if the loss caused or the value of what is obtained or sought to be obtained does not exceed $500, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: False statement by promoter, etc
sections.fullText: 242 False statement by promoter, etc 1 Every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 10 years who, in respect of any body, whether incorporated or unincorporated and whether formed or intended to be formed, makes or concurs in making or publishes any false statementwith intent— a to induce any person, whether ascertained or not, to acquire any financial product within the meaning of the; or b to deceive or cause loss to any person, whether ascertained or not; or c to induce any person, whether ascertained or not, to entrust or advance any property to any other person. 2 In this section,means any statement in respect of which the person making or publishing the statement— a knows the statement is false in a material particular; or b is reckless as to whether the statement is false in a material particular. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2013 No 70). :, on, byof the(2013 No 70).

sections.heading: Money laundering
sections.fullText: 243 Money laundering 1 For the purposes of this section and,— includes an omission , in relation to property, means to conceal or disguise the property; and includes, without limitation,— a to convert the property from one form to another: b to conceal or disguise the nature, source, location, disposition, or ownership of the property or of any interest in the property , in relation to property, means to deal with the property in any manner and by any means; and includes, without limitation,— a to dispose of the property, whether by way of sale, purchase, gift, or otherwise: b to transfer possession of the property: c to bring the property into New Zealand: d to remove the property from New Zealand , in relation to property, means— a a legal or equitable estate or interest in the property; or b a right, power, or privilege in connection with the property means an offence (or any offence described as a crime) that is punishable under New Zealand law, including any act, wherever committed, that would be an offence in New Zealand if committed in New Zealand , in relation to, means any property that is derived or realised, directly or indirectly, by any person from the commission of the offence means real or personal property of any description, whether situated in New Zealand or elsewhere and whether tangible or intangible; and includes an interest in any such real or personal property. 2 Subject to, every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years who, in respect of any property that is the proceeds of, engages in a money laundering transaction, knowing or believing that all or part of the property is the proceeds of, or being reckless as to whether or not the property is the proceeds of. 3 Subject to, every one is liable to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 5 years who obtains or has in his or her possession any property (being property that is the proceeds ofcommitted by another person)— a with intent to engage in a money laundering transaction in respect of that property; and b knowing or believing that all or part of the property is the proceeds of, or being reckless as to whether or not the property is the proceeds of. 4 For the purposes of this section, a person engages in a money laundering transaction if,that person— a deals with that property; or b assists any other person, whether directly or indirectly, to deal with that property. 4A Despite anything in subsection (4), the prosecution is not required to prove that the defendant had an intent to— a conceal any property; or b enable any person to conceal any property. 5 In any prosecution for an offence against subsection (2) or subsection (3),— a it is not necessary for the prosecution to prove that theknew or believed that the property was the proceeds of a particularoffence or a particular class ofoffence: b it is no defence that thebelieved any property to be the proceeds of a particularoffence when in fact the property was the proceeds of anotheroffence. 6 Nothing in this section or inlimits or restricts the operation of any other provision of this Act or any other enactment. 7 To avoid doubt, for the purposes of the definition of offence in subsection (1), New Zealand law includes, but is not limited to, the. 1961 No 43, 257A(1)–(5) :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2011 No 85). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95).

sections.heading: Charges for money laundering
sections.fullText: 243A Charges for money laundering A person may be charged underin respect of any property that is the proceeds of an offence to which section 243(2) or (3) applies even though the person who committed the offence— a has not been charged with that offence; or b has not been convicted of that offence. :, on, byof the(2015 No 95).

sections.heading: Defence of enforcement of enactment
sections.fullText: 244 Defence of enforcement of enactment It is a defence to a charge underif the person charged proves that the act to which the charge relates was done by that person, in good faith, for the purpose of, or in connection with,— a the enforcement or intended enforcement of; or b the enforcement or intended enforcement of the; or ba the enforcement or intended enforcement of the; or c the enforcement or intended enforcement of the. 1961 No 43 s 257A(6) :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2015 No 95). :, on, byof the(2009 No 8). :, on, byof the(2009 No 35).

sections.heading: Application of section 243 to acts outside New Zealand
sections.fullText: 245 Application of section 243 to acts outside New Zealand 1 applies to an act that has occurred outside New Zealand and that is alleged to constitute an offence resulting in proceeds only if— a the act was an offence under the law of the place where and when it occurred; or b it is an act to whichof this Act applies; or c an enactment provides that the act is an offence in New Zealand, and no additional requirement exists for the act to be an offence in the place where and when it occurred. 2 If a person is charged with an offence underand subsection (1) (a) applies, it is to be presumed, unless that person puts the matter at issue, that the act was an offence under the law of the place where and when it occurred. :, on, byof the(2015 No 95).

sections.heading: Receiving
sections.fullText: 246 Receiving 1 Every one is guilty of receiving who receives any property stolen or obtained by any other, knowing that property to have been stolen or so obtained, or being reckless as to whether or not the property had been stolen or so obtained. 2 For the purposes of this section, property that was obtained by any act committed outside New Zealand that, if it had been committed in New Zealand, would have constitutedis, subject to subsection (5), to be regarded as having been obtained by. 3 The act of receiving any property stolen or obtained by any otheris complete as soon as the offender has, either exclusively or jointly with the thief or any other person, possession of, or control over, the property or helps in concealing or disposing of the property. 4 If— a subsequent receiving of it is not an offence, even though the receiver may know that the property had previously been stolen or obtained by any other. a any property stolen or obtained by any otherhas been returned to the owner; or b legal title to any such property has been acquired by any person,— 5 If a person is charged with an offence under this section and the property was obtained by an act committed outside New Zealand, it is to be presumed, unless the person charged puts the matter at issue, that the doing of the act by which the property was obtained was an offence under the law of the place where the act was done. 1961 No 43,, :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27). :, on, byof the(2013 No 27).

sections.heading: Punishment of receiving
sections.fullText: 247 Punishment of receiving Every person who is guilty of receiving is liable as follows: a if the value of the property received exceeds $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 7 years: b if the value of the property received exceeds $500 but does not exceed the sum of $1,000, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 1 year: c if the value of the property received does not exceed $500, to imprisonment for a term not exceeding 3 months. 1961 No 43 :, on, byof the(2003 No 39).

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.fullText: 248 Interpretation For the purposes of this section and,— , in relation to any computer system, means instruct, communicate with, store data in, receive data from, or otherwise make use of any of the resources of the computer system includes an authorisation conferred on a person by or under an enactment or a rule of law, or by an order of a court or judicial process — a means— i a computer; or ii 2 or more interconnected computers; or iii any communication links between computers or to remote terminals or another device; or iv 2 or more interconnected computers combined with any communication links between computers or to remote terminals or any other device; and b includes any part of the items described in paragraph (a) and all related input, output, processing, storage, software, or communication facilities, and stored data. :, on, byof the(2003 No 39). :, on, byof the(2025 No 39). :, on, byof the(2011 No 29).
